content.text:
# Start Task - CLAUDE.md Compliance Reminder

**This is an automated reminder to follow the CLAUDE.md workflow.**

---

## 🚨 BEFORE YOU START CODING

### Step 1: Sync with Main Branch (Rule #11)
```bash
git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit
```

### Step 2: Read CLAUDE.md (Rules #1-11)
- Open and review `CLAUDE.md` in the root directory
- Pay special attention to rules #1-11 (IMMUTABLE)
- Review Core Development Principles (Privacy, Security, UX, Performance)

### Step 3: Create Task File (Rule #1)
```bash
# Create new task file using template
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
```

**Replace `[feature-name]` with descriptive name:**
- ✅ Good: `tasks/add-search-functionality.md`
- ✅ Good: `tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md`
- ❌ Bad: `tasks/todo.md`
- ❌ Bad: `tasks/task1.md`

### Step 4: Fill Out Task Plan (Rule #2)
Open `tasks/[feature-name].md` and complete:

**Required Sections:**
- [ ] **Overview** - What problem are you solving?
- [ ] **Problem Statement** - Detailed explanation
- [ ] **Files to Read** - List all files you need to understand
- [ ] **Proposed Changes** - Detailed technical plan
- [ ] **Todo Checklist** - Break down work into specific, actionable items with time estimates

**Use the workflow checklist for guidance:**
```bash
# Reference this while planning
.claude/workflow-checklist.md
```

### Step 5: Get User Approval (Rule #3)
⚠️ **CRITICAL: DO NOT START CODING UNTIL USER APPROVES PLAN**

Use the `ExitPlanMode` tool to present your plan and get approval.

### Step 6: Begin Implementation (Rule #4)
Only after user approval:
- Mark tasks as `in_progress` as you work
- Mark tasks as `completed` immediately when done
- Update the todo list frequently (not in batches)

### Step 7: Add Review Section When Done (Rule #7)
When ALL work is complete, add comprehensive review section to task file:
- Summary of changes
- Files created/modified (with line numbers)
- Testing status
- Issues encountered
- Time comparison (estimated vs actual)

### Step 8: Update Documentation (Rule #10)
If you made architectural changes, update:
- `docs/technical/` - New systems, patterns, architecture
- `docs/product/feature_map.md` - New features shipped
- Any other relevant docs

### Step 9: Run Tests & Checks
```bash
npm run type-check    # TypeScript check
npm run lint          # ESLint check
npm run build         # Verify bundle size < 100KB
```

---

## 📋 Quick Checklist

- [ ] Synced with main branch (`git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit`)
- [ ] Read CLAUDE.md rules #1-11
- [ ] Created task file: `tasks/[feature-name].md`
- [ ] Filled out all required sections
- [ ] Got user approval via `ExitPlanMode` tool
- [ ] **ONLY THEN** begin coding

---

## 📚 Resources

- **Full Workflow:** See `CLAUDE.md` in root directory
- **Detailed Checklist:** See `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
- **Task Template:** See `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
- **Automation Guide:** See `.claude/automation-guide.md`

---

## 🔧 Automation Tools

**Git Pre-Commit Hook:**
- Automatically blocks commits without task file updates
- Located at `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
- Emergency bypass: `git commit --no-verify` (use sparingly!)

**Custom Commands:**
- `/start-task` - This reminder (you're here!)
- Add more commands as needed in `.claude/commands/`

---

## ⚠️ Common Mistakes to Avoid

1. ❌ Starting to code without creating task file (Rule #1)
2. ❌ Starting to code without user approval (Rule #3)
3. ❌ Forgetting to add review section when done (Rule #7)
4. ❌ Not updating documentation for architectural changes (Rule #10)
5. ❌ Not syncing with main at start of session (Rule #11)
6. ❌ Being lazy - always find root cause, no temporary fixes (Rule #8)
7. ❌ Making complex changes - keep it simple, minimal code impact (Rule #9)

---

## 🎯 Remember the Core Rules

1. **Think → Plan → Write to tasks/[feature].md** - Create task file FIRST
2. **Plan has todo list** - Checklist with items to check off
3. **Check with me before working** - Get approval via ExitPlanMode
4. **Begin working, mark tasks complete** - Update todos as you go
5. **High-level explanations only** - Don't overexplain every detail
6. **Make changes as simple as possible** - Minimal code impact
7. **Add review section** when done - Document what changed
8. **DO NOT BE LAZY** - Find root cause, no temporary fixes
9. **Simplicity is key** - Impact minimal code, avoid bugs
10. **Update documentation** - Keep docs detailed, clear, clean
11. **Sync with main** at start of EVERY session - Stay up to date

---

**Ready to start? Follow the checklist above!** ✅

     7→ * AI Model metadata

     9→export interface AIModel {
    10→  id: string; // Unique model identifier (e.g., "gpt-4o", "llama-3.3-70b")
    11→  name: string; // Display name (e.g., "GPT-4o", "Llama 3.3 70B")
    12→  provider: string; // Provider ID (e.g., "openai", "groq")
    13→

    16→  qualityRating: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5; // 1 = basic, 5 = excellent
    17→
    18→  // Capabilities
    19→  contextWindow: number; // Max tokens in context (e.g., 128000)
    20→  maxOutputTokens?: number; // Max tokens in response
    21→  supportsStreaming: boolean; // Can stream responses
    22→  supportsVision?: boolean; // Can process images
    23→  supportsFunctionCalling?: boolean; // Can call functions
    24→
    25→  // Cost and availability
    26→  isFree: boolean; // Available on free tier

    28→  requiresApiKey: boolean; // Needs API key
    29→
    30→  // Use cases

    32→  description?: string; // Brief description of model strengths

    36→ * AI Provider metadata
    37→ */
    38→export interface AIProviderMetadata {
    39→  id: string; // Unique provider ID (e.g., "openai", "groq")
    40→  name: string; // Display name (e.g., "OpenAI", "Groq")
    41→  displayName: string; // Branded name for UI
    42→  description: string; // Brief description
    43→
    44→  // Authentication
    45→  requiresApiKey: boolean; // Needs API key
    46→  apiKeyUrl?: string; // Where to get API key
    47→  apiKeyLabel?: string; // Label for API key input (e.g., "OpenAI API Key")
    48→
    49→  // Availability
    50→  hasFreeModels: boolean; // Has free tier models
    51→  freeModelIds?: string[]; // IDs of free models
    52→
    53→  // Rate limits (free tier)
    54→  freeTierLimits?: {
    55→    requestsPerMinute?: number;
    56→    requestsPerDay?: number;
    57→    tokensPerMinute?: number;
    58→    tokensPerDay?: number;
    59→    description?: string; // Human-readable description
    60→  };
    61→
    62→  // Features
    63→  supportsCORS?: boolean; // Can be called from browser
    64→  requiresProxy?: boolean; // Needs backend proxy
    65→  supportsStreaming: boolean; // Can stream responses
    66→
    67→  // Branding
    68→  logoUrl?: string; // Provider logo
    69→  websiteUrl?: string; // Provider website
    70→  docsUrl?: string; // API documentation
    71→}
    72→
    73→/**
    74→ * AI message options
    75→ */
    76→export interface AIMessageOptions {
    77→  prompt: string; // User message
    78→  conversationHistory?: Array<{ role: 'user' | 'assistant' | 'system'; content: string }>; // Previous messages
    79→  systemPrompt?: string; // System instruction
    80→  temperature?: number; // 0-1, creativity level
    81→  maxTokens?: number; // Max response length
    82→  stream?: boolean; // Enable streaming
    83→  onChunk?: (chunk: string) => void; // Stream callback
    84→}
    85→
    86→/**
    87→ * AI response
    88→ */
    89→export interface AIResponse {
    90→  content: string; // Response text
    91→  model: string; // Model used
    92→  provider: string; // Provider used
    93→  finishReason?: 'stop' | 'length' | 'error'; // Why generation stopped
    94→  usage?: {
    95→    promptTokens: number;
    96→    completionTokens: number;
    97→    totalTokens: number;
    98→  };
    99→}
   100→
   101→/**
   102→ * Provider error types
   103→ */
   104→export const ProviderErrorType = {
   105→  INVALID_API_KEY: 'invalid_api_key',
   106→  RATE_LIMIT: 'rate_limit',
   107→  QUOTA_EXCEEDED: 'quota_exceeded',
   108→  MODEL_NOT_FOUND: 'model_not_found',
   109→  NETWORK_ERROR: 'network_error',
   110→  TIMEOUT: 'timeout',
   111→  UNKNOWN: 'unknown',
   112→} as const;
   113→
   114→export type ProviderErrorType = typeof ProviderErrorType[keyof typeof ProviderErrorType];
   115→
   116→/**
   117→ * Provider error
   118→ */
   119→export class ProviderError extends Error {
   120→  type: ProviderErrorType;
   121→  provider: string;
   122→  retryable: boolean;
   123→
   124→  constructor(
   125→    type: ProviderErrorType,
   126→    message: string,
   127→    provider: string,
   128→    retryable: boolean = false
   129→  ) {
   130→    super(message);
   131→    this.name = 'ProviderError';
   132→    this.type = type;
   133→    this.provider = provider;
   134→    this.retryable = retryable;
   135→  }
   136→}
   137→
   138→/**
   139→ * AI Provider interface
   140→ * All provider implementations must implement this interface
   141→ */
   142→export interface AIProvider {
   143→  // Metadata
   144→  metadata: AIProviderMetadata;
   145→  models: AIModel[];
   146→
   147→  // Configuration
   148→  isConfigured(): boolean; // Has valid API key
   149→  setApiKey(apiKey: string): void;
   150→  getApiKey(): string | null;
   151→  clearApiKey(): void;
   152→
   153→  // Validation
   154→  validateApiKey(apiKey: string): Promise<boolean>;
   155→
   156→  // Model operations
   157→  getModel(modelId: string): AIModel | null;
   158→  getDefaultModel(): AIModel;
   159→  getFreeModels(): AIModel[];
   160→
   161→  // Messaging
   162→  sendMessage(model: string, options: AIMessageOptions): Promise<AIResponse>;
   163→
   164→  // Usage tracking
   165→  getUsage?(): Promise<{ requestsToday: number; tokensToday: number; limitReached: boolean }>;
   166→}
   167→
   168→/**
   169→ * Provider selection criteria
   170→ */
   171→export interface ProviderSelectionCriteria {
   172→  requireFree?: boolean; // Only free models
   173→  requireFast?: boolean; // Prioritize speed
   174→  requireHighQuality?: boolean; // Prioritize quality
   175→  minContextWindow?: number; // Minimum context size
   176→  useCase?: string; // Specific use case
   177→}
   178→
   179→/**
   180→ * Provider router configuration
   181→ */
   182→export interface ProviderRouterConfig {
   183→  primaryProvider: string; // Primary provider ID
   184→  primaryModel: string; // Primary model ID
   185→  fallbackEnabled: boolean; // Enable automatic fallbacks
   186→  fallbackOrder: string[]; // Provider IDs in fallback order
   187→  notifyOnFallback: boolean; // Show notification when falling back
   188→}
   189→
   190→/**
   191→ * Usage statistics
   192→ */
   193→export interface ProviderUsageStats {

   195→  model: string;
   196→  requestsToday: number;
   197→  tokensToday: number;
   198→  lastRequestAt: Date | null;
   199→  limitReached: boolean;

input.content:
# Task: Add DeepSeek AI Provider

## Overview
Add DeepSeek as a new AI provider option. DeepSeek offers high-quality LLMs (including DeepSeek-V3 and DeepSeek-Coder) with an OpenAI-compatible API, making integration straightforward.

## Problem Statement
The user wants to use DeepSeek models in the Neuman Brain dashboard. DeepSeek is known for:
- Strong reasoning capabilities (DeepSeek-V3 rivals GPT-4)
- Excellent coding performance (DeepSeek-Coder)
- Competitive pricing
- OpenAI-compatible API

## Files to Read
- [x] `src/services/ai/types.ts` - AIProvider interface and types
- [x] `src/services/ai/providerRouter.ts` - Provider registry
- [x] `src/services/ai/xaiProvider.ts` - Reference implementation (uses OpenAI SDK)

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Create `src/services/ai/deepseekProvider.ts`
Following the xAI provider pattern (OpenAI SDK compatible):
- DeepSeek API base URL: `https://api.deepseek.com`
- Models: DeepSeek-V3, DeepSeek-Coder-V2
- BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) - requires user API key

### 2. Update `src/services/ai/providerRouter.ts`
- Import deepseekProvider
- Add to PROVIDERS registry

## Todo Checklist

- [ ] Create `deepseekProvider.ts` with:
  - [ ] Provider metadata (id, name, apiKeyUrl, etc.)
  - [ ] Model definitions (deepseek-chat, deepseek-coder)
  - [ ] DeepSeekProvider class implementing AIProvider
  - [ ] Singleton export
- [ ] Register in providerRouter.ts PROVIDERS object
- [ ] Run type-check and build to verify

## DeepSeek API Details

**Base URL:** `https://api.deepseek.com`
**API Key URL:** `https://platform.deepseek.com/api_keys`
**Docs:** `https://platform.deepseek.com/docs`

### Models
| Model ID | Description | Context | Speed | Quality |
|----------|-------------|---------|-------|---------|
| deepseek-chat | DeepSeek-V3 chat model | 64K | 4 | 5 |
| deepseek-coder | DeepSeek-Coder-V2 for code | 128K | 4 | 5 |
| deepseek-reasoner | DeepSeek-R1 reasoning model | 64K | 3 | 5 |

### Pricing (as of Jan 2025)
- Input: ~$0.14/1M tokens
- Output: ~$0.28/1M tokens
(Very competitive compared to GPT-4)

## Review Section
(To be completed after implementation)

## Review Section

### Summary
Successfully added DeepSeek as a new AI provider option.

### Files Created/Modified
- **Created:** `src/services/ai/deepseekProvider.ts` (268 lines)
  - Provider metadata with API key URL, docs, etc.
  - 3 models: deepseek-chat (V3), deepseek-coder (Coder-V2), deepseek-reasoner (R1)
  - Full AIProvider implementation using OpenAI SDK
  - Error handling mapped to ProviderError types

- **Modified:** `src/services/ai/providerRouter.ts`
  - Added import for deepseekProvider (line 26)
  - Added to PROVIDERS registry (line 51)

### Testing Status
- Type-check: PASSED
- Build: PASSED (bundle size within limits)

### Usage
Users can now:
1. Add their DeepSeek API key in settings
2. Select DeepSeek models (V3, Coder-V2, R1) for AI conversations
3. Get API key from: https://platform.deepseek.com/api_keys

# Task: Add DeepSeek AI Provider

## Overview
Add DeepSeek as a new AI provider option. DeepSeek offers high-quality LLMs (including DeepSeek-V3 and DeepSeek-Coder) with an OpenAI-compatible API, making integration straightforward.

## Problem Statement
The user wants to use DeepSeek models in the Neuman Brain dashboard. DeepSeek is known for:
- Strong reasoning capabilities (DeepSeek-V3 rivals GPT-4)
- Excellent coding performance (DeepSeek-Coder)
- Competitive pricing
- OpenAI-compatible API

## Files to Read
- [x] `src/services/ai/types.ts` - AIProvider interface and types
- [x] `src/services/ai/providerRouter.ts` - Provider registry
- [x] `src/services/ai/xaiProvider.ts` - Reference implementation (uses OpenAI SDK)

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Create `src/services/ai/deepseekProvider.ts`
Following the xAI provider pattern (OpenAI SDK compatible):
- DeepSeek API base URL: `https://api.deepseek.com`
- Models: DeepSeek-V3, DeepSeek-Coder-V2
- BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) - requires user API key

### 2. Update `src/services/ai/providerRouter.ts`
- Import deepseekProvider
- Add to PROVIDERS registry

## Todo Checklist

- [ ] Create `deepseekProvider.ts` with:
  - [ ] Provider metadata (id, name, apiKeyUrl, etc.)
  - [ ] Model definitions (deepseek-chat, deepseek-coder)
  - [ ] DeepSeekProvider class implementing AIProvider
  - [ ] Singleton export
- [ ] Register in providerRouter.ts PROVIDERS object
- [ ] Run type-check and build to verify

## DeepSeek API Details

**Base URL:** `https://api.deepseek.com`
**API Key URL:** `https://platform.deepseek.com/api_keys`
**Docs:** `https://platform.deepseek.com/docs`

### Models
| Model ID | Description | Context | Speed | Quality |
|----------|-------------|---------|-------|---------|
| deepseek-chat | DeepSeek-V3 chat model | 64K | 4 | 5 |
| deepseek-coder | DeepSeek-Coder-V2 for code | 128K | 4 | 5 |
| deepseek-reasoner | DeepSeek-R1 reasoning model | 64K | 3 | 5 |

### Pricing (as of Jan 2025)
- Input: ~$0.14/1M tokens
- Output: ~$0.28/1M tokens
(Very competitive compared to GPT-4)

## Review Section
(To be completed after implementation)

lines: +Successfully added DeepSeek as a new AI provider option.
+  - Provider metadata with API key URL, docs, etc.
+  - Full AIProvider implementation using OpenAI SDK
+  - Error handling mapped to ProviderError types
+1. Add their DeepSeek API key in settings
+2. Select DeepSeek models (V3, Coder-V2, R1) for AI conversations

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed exploration report of the AI Terminal and Phantom Shell implementation:

## Exploration Report: AI Terminal & Phantom Shell Implementation

Based on my thorough analysis of the Neuman Brain Dashboard codebase, here's a comprehensive understanding of the terminal architecture:

---

### 1. HOW THE TERMINAL WORKS (Overall Architecture)

The terminal system has **two distinct modes** accessed through the AITerminal component:

#### **Mode 1: AI Chat (Default)**
- **File:** `src/components/AITerminal.tsx` (725 lines)
- **Purpose:** Multi-provider AI assistant chat interface
- **Providers Supported:** 8 providers (OpenRouter, Groq, HuggingFace, Mistral, Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI)
- **Key Features:**
  - Streaming responses with real-time content display
  - Provider/model selection with fallback support
  - Usage tracking and encrypted API key storage
  - Markdown rendering with sanitization (XSS protection)
  - Command: `Ctrl+Shift+A` / `Cmd+Shift+A` to toggle

#### **Mode 2: Phantom Shell (Development Terminal)**
- **Files:** `src/components/PhantomShell/Terminal.tsx` and `Preview.tsx`
- **Purpose:** Real terminal emulator for development in browser
- **Technology:** xterm.js (full ANSI color support, clickable URLs, search)
- **Key Features:**
  - Real xterm.js terminal emulator (not a chat interface)
  - Command history with arrow navigation
  - Live preview pane for dev server output
  - Browser-native development (no installation needed)

---

### 2. WHAT IS WEBCONTAINER & WHY IT'S NEEDED

**WebContainer** is a **Node.js runtime in the browser** using WebAssembly. Here's why it's critical:

#### **What It Does:**
- Runs real Node.js commands (`npm`, `node`, `npx`, `ls`, `cd`, etc.)
- Provides in-memory file system (persisted to IndexedDB)
- Spawns actual processes and captures their output/exit codes
- Listens for dev server startup and forwards to preview pane

#### **Why Commands Like `ping` Need It:**
```
Traditional browser terminal: Only shows input/output (mock)
WebContainer terminal: Actually runs processes in browser
```

#### **Technical Requirements (Critical):**
```typescript
// From webContainerService.ts
- SharedArrayBuffer support (required for WebAssembly)
- Cross-Origin Isolation headers (COEP/COOP in Vite config)
- Supported browsers: Chrome 89+, Edge 89+, Firefox 89+, Safari 16.4+
```

---

### 3. THE ERROR MESSAGE & WHY IT APPEARS

**Error:** `"WebContainer not ready. Boot it first with /version"`

**Where It Appears:** `src/services/phantomShell/shellService.ts` (line 258)

**Why It Happens:**
```typescript
// When trying to execute a system command (npm, node, etc.)
if (!webcontainer) {
  // WebContainer not booted yet (takes 2-5 seconds)
  onStderr('\x1b[33m⚠ WebContainer not ready. Boot it first with /version\x1b[0m\n');
  return 1;
}
```

**How to Fix It:**
1. **Automatic:** Running any command triggers boot if not already booted
2. **Manual:** Type `/version` to check WebContainer status and trigger boot if needed
3. **What Happens:** WebContainer.boot() is called, shows loading state, then ready

**Lifecycle:**
```
1. User runs command (e.g., npm install)
2. webContainerService checks if booted (null check)
3. If not booted, either:
   - Shows error (user should run /version)
   - Auto-boots on /version command
4. Boot takes 2-5 seconds (shown in UI)
5. Once booted, all commands work normally
```

---

### 4. AVAILABLE COMMANDS & HOW THEY WORK

#### **Built-in Commands** (Prefix with `/`)

**Help & System:**
```
/help          → Show all available commands
/version       → Boot WebContainer + show version/status
/about         → About Phantom Shell
/clear         → Clear terminal screen
/history       → Show recent command history (last 20)
```

**Project Management:**
```
/projects      → List all projects
/new <name>    → Create new project with template
/open <name>   → Open/activate project
/close         → Close active project
/export        → Export project to .brain file (coming soon)
```

**AI Integration:**
```
/ai <prompt>   → Ask AI assistant (e.g., /ai create react app)
                → Uses same provider router as chat mode
                → Temperature: 0.3 (more deterministic)
                → Max tokens: 500
```

#### **System Commands** (WebContainer)

Only available **after WebContainer boots**. Common examples:
```bash
node -v              # Check Node.js version
npm init -y          # Initialize project
npm install lodash   # Install package
npm run dev          # Run dev server
node index.js        # Execute script
ls -la              # List files
cd /directory        # Change directory
cat file.txt         # View file
mkdir folder         # Create directory
```

#### **Command Parsing Flow** (`parseCommand` function):

```typescript
1. Check if starts with /ai   → type: 'ai'
2. Check if starts with /     → type: 'builtin' (if recognized)
3. Otherwise                  → type: 'system' (needs WebContainer)
```

---

### 5. COMMAND EXECUTION ARCHITECTURE

**Three Command Types:**

```typescript
// From shellService.ts

// 1. BUILTIN (Instant, no WebContainer needed)
type: 'builtin' → executeBuiltin()
  ├─ Calls handler from builtinHandlers object
  ├─ Returns formatted string (ANSI colors included)
  └─ Always succeeds immediately

// 2. AI (Uses provider router, streaming)
type: 'ai' → executeAI()
  ├─ Gets shared router from AITerminal
  ├─ Uses processAICommand from aiCommandService.ts
  ├─ Shows thinking indicator
  ├─ Parses response into commands + explanation
  └─ Max 500 tokens, temperature 0.3

// 3. SYSTEM (WebContainer process spawning)
type: 'system' → executeSystem()
  ├─ Gets WebContainer instance
  ├─ Spawns process: webcontainer.spawn(command, args)
  ├─ Streams stdout/stderr in real-time
  ├─ Returns exit code when done
  └─ Handles errors gracefully
```

**Example: Running `npm install lodash`**

```
Input: "npm install lodash"
  ↓
Parse: type='system', name='npm', args=['install', 'lodash']
  ↓
Check WebContainer booted? → YES (or wait)
  ↓
spawn('npm', ['install', 'lodash'])
  ↓
Stream output to terminal in real-time
  ↓
Wait for process.exit promise
  ↓
Return exitCode (0 = success)
```

---

### 6. AI INTEGRATION WITH PHANTOM SHELL

**Two-Step Process:**

```typescript
// File: src/services/phantomShell/aiCommandService.ts

Step 1: TEMPLATE MATCHING (Fast)
├─ Templates like "create react app" → instant match
├─ Returns pre-defined command
└─ No AI call needed

Step 2: AI REQUEST (If no template)
├─ Get shared router from AITerminal
├─ Send prompt with SYSTEM_PROMPT context
├─ System prompt explains WebContainer limitations:
│  - Available: npm, node, npx, basic shell
│  - NOT available: git, docker, python
├─ Parse response for commands vs explanation
└─ Return array of commands to execute
```

**System Prompt Context:**
```
"You are Phantom Shell AI, a terminal assistant 
for a browser-based development environment.

CONTEXT:
- You are running in WebContainers (Node.js in browser)
- Available: npm, npx, node, basic shell commands
- NOT available: git, docker, python, system-level commands
- File system is in-memory (persisted to IndexedDB)"
```

---

### 7. STATE MANAGEMENT (Zustand Store)

**File:** `src/stores/usePhantomShellStore.ts`

**Key State:**
```typescript
PhantomShellState {
  // Terminal
  terminalInstance: XTerm | null           // Active xterm instance
  isTerminalReady: boolean                  // Terminal initialized

  // WebContainer
  isWebContainerReady: boolean              // Booted and ready
  isWebContainerBooting: boolean            // Currently booting
  webContainerError: string | null          // Boot error message

  // Projects
  projects: Record<string, PhantomProject>  // All projects
  activeProjectId: string | null            // Currently open project

  // Command History
  commandHistory: CommandHistoryEntry[]     // Full history
  historyIndex: number                      // Navigation position

  // Dev Server
  devServerUrl: string | null               // Running dev server URL
  devServerPort: number | null              // Port number
  isDevServerRunning: boolean               // Server status

  // UI
  showPreview: boolean                      // Preview pane visibility
  splitRatio: number                        // Terminal:Preview ratio
}
```

**Key Actions:**
- Terminal: setTerminalInstance, setTerminalReady
- WebContainer: setWebContainerReady, setWebContainerBooting, setWebContainerError
- Projects: createProject, updateProject, deleteProject, setActiveProject
- History: addToHistory, navigateHistory (up/down arrows), clearHistory
- Dev Server: setDevServer, setDevServerRunning (triggered by 'server-ready' event)

---

### 8. TERMINAL RENDERING (xterm.js)

**File:** `src/components/PhantomShell/Terminal.tsx`

**Theme Configuration:**
```typescript
THEME = {
  background: '#0a0a0f',      // Dark surface
  foreground: '#e4e4e7',      // Light text
  cursor: '#d946ef',          // Magenta accent
  // Full ANSI color palette for 256-color support
  // Green for success, Red for errors, Cyan for secondary
}
```

**Features:**
- FitAddon: Auto-resize to container
- WebLinksAddon: Clickable URLs
- SearchAddon: Ctrl+Shift+F to find
- ResizeObserver: Dynamic height adjustment
- convertEol: true (critical for line handling)

**Keyboard Handlers:**
```
Enter → Execute command (handled in AITerminal)
Backspace → Delete character
Ctrl+C → Cancel input
Up/Down → Navigate history
Regular chars → Echo to terminal
```

---

### 9. DATA FLOW: TERMINAL INPUT → EXECUTION → OUTPUT

```
User types in terminal
          ↓
onData callback (AITerminal.tsx line 198)
          ↓
handleTerminalData processes raw input
  ├─ Enter key → Run command
  ├─ Backspace → Delete character
  ├─ Ctrl+C → Cancel
  ├─ Arrow keys → Navigate history
  └─ Regular char → Echo & update shellInput state
          ↓
runCommand() from shellService.ts
          ↓
parseCommand() determines type
          ↓
Branch by type:
  ├─ builtin → executeBuiltin() → Instant response
  ├─ ai → executeAI() → AI provider call
  └─ system → executeSystem() → WebContainer spawn
          ↓
Output piped to terminal.write()
          ↓
Terminal re-renders in real-time
          ↓
Show exit code + new prompt
```

---

### 10. EXISTING DOCUMENTATION

**User-Facing Docs:**
- **File:** `docs/product/phantom-shell-guide.md` (191 lines)
- **Coverage:** Commands, keyboard shortcuts, browser requirements, troubleshooting
- **Status:** Comprehensive but missing technical architecture details

**Code Documentation:**
- JSDoc comments in all service files
- Type definitions exported for clarity
- Logger module with structured logging
- No separate architecture document (opportunity for improvement)

---

### 11. KEY FILES REFERENCE MAP

| File | Purpose | Lines | Key Exports |
|------|---------|-------|------------|
| `src/components/AITerminal.tsx` | Main terminal UI (chat + shell modes) | 725 | AITerminal component |
| `src/components/PhantomShell/Terminal.tsx` | xterm.js wrapper | 183 | Terminal component |
| `src/components/PhantomShell/Preview.tsx` | Dev server preview iframe | 71 | Preview component |
| `src/services/phantomShell/shellService.ts` | Command parsing & execution | 427 | parseCommand, runCommand, ShellExecutor |
| `src/services/phantomShell/webContainerService.ts` | WebContainer lifecycle | 353 | bootWebContainer, getWebContainer, mountFileSystem |
| `src/services/phantomShell/aiCommandService.ts` | AI command generation | 226 | processAICommand, tryTemplate |
| `src/stores/usePhantomShellStore.ts` | State management | 200+ | PhantomShellState, PhantomShellActions |
| `docs/product/phantom-shell-guide.md` | User guide | 191 | User-facing documentation |

---

### 12. CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

**WebContainer Singleton Pattern:**
```typescript
// Only one instance per session (expensive to boot)
let webcontainerInstance: WebContainer | null = null;
let bootPromise: Promise<WebContainer> | null = null;

// Boot triggers: /version command or first system command
// Duration: 2-5 seconds
// Listeners: 'server-ready' event for dev servers
```

**Real-Time Output Streaming:**
```typescript
// WebContainer process output piped via WritableStream
process.output.pipeTo(new WritableStream({
  write(data) {
    terminal.write(data);  // Real-time display
  }
}));

// Exit code awaited
const exitCode = await process.exit;
```

**Command History Navigation:**
```typescript
// Up/Down arrows trigger navigateHistory('up'|'down')
// Stores last position (historyIndex) in Zustand
// Can show previous/next commands without re-executing
```

**AI Provider Sharing:**
```typescript
// AITerminal creates router with all providers
// AICommandService uses same router via setRouter()
// Ensures consistent API keys and fallback behavior
```

---

### 13. BROWSER ISOLATION & SECURITY

**Cross-Origin Isolation (Required for WebContainer):**
```
Headers needed:
- Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin (COOP)
- Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy: require-corp (COEP)

Enables:
- SharedArrayBuffer (WebAssembly feature)
- High-precision timers
- Nested workers

Without these: "WebContainer requires Cross-Origin Isolation"
```

**Content Security:**
```typescript
// Phantom Shell output uses ANSI escape codes only
// No HTML rendering (prevents XSS)

// AI responses use react-markdown + rehype-sanitize
sanitizeSchema = {
  // Allows: p, h1-h6, code, pre, lists, tables, links
  // Blocks: script, iframe, onclick, dangerous attrs
  // Link protocols: http, https, mailto only
}
```

---

### 14. PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS

| Metric | Value | Notes |
|--------|-------|-------|
| Bundle size (Phantom Shell) | ~200KB | Lazy-loaded |
| WebContainer boot time | 2-5 seconds | One-time per session |
| Terminal render latency | <50ms | Real-time streaming |
| Command history storage | 1000 entries | In-memory + IndexedDB |
| File system capacity | 50GB+ | IndexedDB limit |
| Concurrent processes | Limited | Single WebContainer instance |

---

### 15. CURRENT LIMITATIONS & ROADMAP

**Current Status:**
- Phase 2: WebContainer integration complete
- Phase 3: IndexedDB persistence (implemented)
- Phase 4: AI commands (implemented)
- Phase 5: Live preview pane (implemented)

**Known Limitations:**
- Git not available (plans for isomorphic-git in Phase 6)
- Python/pip not available
- System-level commands not available (chmod, curl via Node)
- File export coming soon

---

## Summary

The Phantom Shell is a **fully-functional development terminal** in the browser using **WebContainers** (Node.js via WebAssembly). The error "WebContainer not ready" simply means the 2-5 second boot process hasn't completed yet. Once booted, it can run real npm/node commands with full output streaming. The AI integration uses the same provider router as the chat mode to generate shell commands from natural language requests.

#### **System Commands** (WebContainer)

Only available **after WebContainer boots**. Common examples:
```bash
node -v              # Check Node.js version
npm init -y          # Initialize project
npm install lodash   # Install package
npm run dev          # Run dev server
node index.js        # Execute script
ls -la              # List files
cd /directory        # Change directory
cat file.txt         # View file
mkdir folder         # Create directory
```

#### **Command Parsing Flow** (`parseCommand` function):

```typescript
1. Check if starts with /ai   → type: 'ai'
2. Check if starts with /     → type: 'builtin' (if recognized)
3. Otherwise                  → type: 'system' (needs WebContainer)
```

---

### 5. COMMAND EXECUTION ARCHITECTURE

**Three Command Types:**

```typescript
// From shellService.ts

// 1. BUILTIN (Instant, no WebContainer needed)
type: 'builtin' → executeBuiltin()
  ├─ Calls handler from builtinHandlers object
  ├─ Returns formatted string (ANSI colors included)
  └─ Always succeeds immediately

// 2. AI (Uses provider router, streaming)
type: 'ai' → executeAI()
  ├─ Gets shared router from AITerminal
  ├─ Uses processAICommand from aiCommandService.ts
  ├─ Shows thinking indicator
  ├─ Parses response into commands + explanation
  └─ Max 500 tokens, temperature 0.3

// 3. SYSTEM (WebContainer process spawning)
type: 'system' → executeSystem()
  ├─ Gets WebContainer instance
  ├─ Spawns process: webcontainer.spawn(command, args)
  ├─ Streams stdout/stderr in real-time
  ├─ Returns exit code when done
  └─ Handles errors gracefully
```

**Example: Running `npm install lodash`**

```
Input: "npm install lodash"
  ↓
Parse: type='system', name='npm', args=['install', 'lodash']
  ↓
Check WebContainer booted? → YES (or wait)
  ↓
spawn('npm', ['install', 'lodash'])
  ↓
Stream output to terminal in real-time
  ↓
Wait for process.exit promise
  ↓
Return exitCode (0 = success)
```

---

### 6. AI INTEGRATION WITH PHANTOM SHELL

**Two-Step Process:**

```typescript
// File: src/services/phantomShell/aiCommandService.ts

Step 1: TEMPLATE MATCHING (Fast)
├─ Templates like "create react app" → instant match
├─ Returns pre-defined command
└─ No AI call needed

Step 2: AI REQUEST (If no template)
├─ Get shared router from AITerminal
├─ Send prompt with SYSTEM_PROMPT context
├─ System prompt explains WebContainer limitations:
│  - Available: npm, node, npx, basic shell
│  - NOT available: git, docker, python
├─ Parse response for commands vs explanation
└─ Return array of commands to execute
```

**System Prompt Context:**
```
"You are Phantom Shell AI, a terminal assistant 
for a browser-based development environment.

CONTEXT:
- You are running in WebContainers (Node.js in browser)
- Available: npm, npx, node, basic shell commands
- NOT available: git, docker, python, system-level commands
- File system is in-memory (persisted to IndexedDB)"
```

---

### 7. STATE MANAGEMENT (Zustand Store)

**File:** `src/stores/usePhantomShellStore.ts`

**Key State:**
```typescript
PhantomShellState {
  // Terminal
  terminalInstance: XTerm | null           // Active xterm instance
  isTerminalReady: boolean                  // Terminal initialized

  // WebContainer
  isWebContainerReady: boolean              // Booted and ready
  isWebContainerBooting: boolean            // Currently booting
  webContainerError: string | null          // Boot error message

  // Projects
  projects: Record<string, PhantomProject>  // All projects
  activeProjectId: string | null            // Currently open project

  // Command History
  commandHistory: CommandHistoryEntry[]     // Full history
  historyIndex: number                      // Navigation position

  // Dev Server
  devServerUrl: string | null               // Running dev server URL
  devServerPort: number | null              // Port number
  isDevServerRunning: boolean               // Server status

  // UI
  showPreview: boolean                      // Preview pane visibility
  splitRatio: number                        // Terminal:Preview ratio
}
```

**Key Actions:**
- Terminal: setTerminalInstance, setTerminalReady
- WebContainer: setWebContainerReady, setWebContainerBooting, setWebContainerError
- Projects: createProject, updateProject, deleteProject, setActiveProject
- History: addToHistory, navigateHistory (up/down arrows), clearHistory
- Dev Server: setDevServer, setDevServerRunning (triggered by 'server-ready' event)

---

### 8. TERMINAL RENDERING (xterm.js)

**File:** `src/components/PhantomShell/Terminal.tsx`

**Theme Configuration:**
```typescript
THEME = {
  background: '#0a0a0f',      // Dark surface
  foreground: '#e4e4e7',      // Light text
  cursor: '#d946ef',          // Magenta accent
  // Full ANSI color palette for 256-color support
  // Green for success, Red for errors, Cyan for secondary
}
```

**Features:**
- FitAddon: Auto-resize to container
- WebLinksAddon: Clickable URLs
- SearchAddon: Ctrl+Shift+F to find
- ResizeObserver: Dynamic height adjustment
- convertEol: true (critical for line handling)

**Keyboard Handlers:**
```
Enter → Execute command (handled in AITerminal)
Backspace → Delete character
Ctrl+C → Cancel input
Up/Down → Navigate history
Regular chars → Echo to terminal
```

---

### 9. DATA FLOW: TERMINAL INPUT → EXECUTION → OUTPUT

```
User types in terminal
          ↓
onData callback (AITerminal.tsx line 198)
          ↓
handleTerminalData processes raw input
  ├─ Enter key → Run command
  ├─ Backspace → Delete character
  ├─ Ctrl+C → Cancel
  ├─ Arrow keys → Navigate history
  └─ Regular char → Echo & update shellInput state
          ↓
runCommand() from shellService.ts
          ↓
parseCommand() determines type
          ↓
Branch by type:
  ├─ builtin → executeBuiltin() → Instant response
  ├─ ai → executeAI() → AI provider call
  └─ system → executeSystem() → WebContainer spawn
          ↓
Output piped to terminal.write()
          ↓
Terminal re-renders in real-time
          ↓
Show exit code + new prompt
```

---

### 10. EXISTING DOCUMENTATION

**User-Facing Docs:**
- **File:** `docs/product/phantom-shell-guide.md` (191 lines)
- **Coverage:** Commands, keyboard shortcuts, browser requirements, troubleshooting
- **Status:** Comprehensive but missing technical architecture details

**Code Documentation:**
- JSDoc comments in all service files
- Type definitions exported for clarity
- Logger module with structured logging
- No separate architecture document (opportunity for improvement)

---

### 11. KEY FILES REFERENCE MAP

| File | Purpose | Lines | Key Exports |
|------|---------|-------|------------|
| `src/components/AITerminal.tsx` | Main terminal UI (chat + shell modes) | 725 | AITerminal component |
| `src/components/PhantomShell/Terminal.tsx` | xterm.js wrapper | 183 | Terminal component |
| `src/components/PhantomShell/Preview.tsx` | Dev server preview iframe | 71 | Preview component |
| `src/services/phantomShell/shellService.ts` | Command parsing & execution | 427 | parseCommand, runCommand, ShellExecutor |
| `src/services/phantomShell/webContainerService.ts` | WebContainer lifecycle | 353 | bootWebContainer, getWebContainer, mountFileSystem |
| `src/services/phantomShell/aiCommandService.ts` | AI command generation | 226 | processAICommand, tryTemplate |
| `src/stores/usePhantomShellStore.ts` | State management | 200+ | PhantomShellState, PhantomShellActions |
| `docs/product/phantom-shell-guide.md` | User guide | 191 | User-facing documentation |

---

### 12. CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

**WebContainer Singleton Pattern:**
```typescript
// Only one instance per session (expensive to boot)
let webcontainerInstance: WebContainer | null = null;
let bootPromise: Promise<WebContainer> | null = null;

// Boot triggers: /version command or first system command
// Duration: 2-5 seconds
// Listeners: 'server-ready' event for dev servers
```

**Real-Time Output Streaming:**
```typescript
// WebContainer process output piped via WritableStream
process.output.pipeTo(new WritableStream({
  write(data) {
    terminal.write(data);  // Real-time display
  }
}));

// Exit code awaited
const exitCode = await process.exit;
```

**Command History Navigation:**
```typescript
// Up/Down arrows trigger navigateHistory('up'|'down')
// Stores last position (historyIndex) in Zustand
// Can show previous/next commands without re-executing
```

**AI Provider Sharing:**
```typescript
// AITerminal creates router with all providers
// AICommandService uses same router via setRouter()
// Ensures consistent API keys and fallback behavior
```

---

### 13. BROWSER ISOLATION & SECURITY

**Cross-Origin Isolation (Required for WebContainer):**
```
Headers needed:
- Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin (COOP)
- Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy: require-corp (COEP)

Enables:
- SharedArrayBuffer (WebAssembly feature)
- High-precision timers
- Nested workers

Without these: "WebContainer requires Cross-Origin Isolation"
```

**Content Security:**
```typescript
// Phantom Shell output uses ANSI escape codes only
// No HTML rendering (prevents XSS)

// AI responses use react-markdown + rehype-sanitize
sanitizeSchema = {
  // Allows: p, h1-h6, code, pre, lists, tables, links
  // Blocks: script, iframe, onclick, dangerous attrs
  // Link protocols: http, https, mailto only
}
```

---

### 14. PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS

| Metric | Value | Notes |
|--------|-------|-------|
| Bundle size (Phantom Shell) | ~200KB | Lazy-loaded |
| WebContainer boot time | 2-5 seconds | One-time per session |
| Terminal render latency | <50ms | Real-time streaming |
| Command history storage | 1000 entries | In-memory + IndexedDB |
| File system capacity | 50GB+ | IndexedDB limit |
| Concurrent processes | Limited | Single WebContainer instance |

---

### 15. CURRENT LIMITATIONS & ROADMAP

**Current Status:**
- Phase 2: WebContainer integration complete
- Phase 3: IndexedDB persistence (implemented)
- Phase 4: AI commands (implemented)
- Phase 5: Live preview pane (implemented)

**Known Limitations:**
- Git not available (plans for isomorphic-git in Phase 6)
- Python/pip not available
- System-level commands not available (chmod, curl via Node)
- File export coming soon

---

## Summary

The Phantom Shell is a **fully-functional development terminal** in the browser using **WebContainers** (Node.js via WebAssembly). The error "WebContainer not ready" simply means the 2-5 second boot process hasn't completed yet. Once booted, it can run real npm/node commands with full output streaming. The AI integration uses the same provider router as the chat mode to generate shell commands from natural language requests.

Perfect! I now have a comprehensive understanding of the "Proxy Required" concept. Let me compile my findings into a detailed exploration report. ## Exploration Report: "Proxy Required" in AI Provider Implementation I've completed a thorough exploration of the AI provider implementation in the Neuman Brain codebase. Here are my findings organized by your search criteria: --- ### 1. Where `requiresProxy` is Defined and Used **Definition Location:** `src/services/ai/types.ts` (lines 63-64) ```typescript export interface AIProviderMetadata { // ... supportsCORS?: boolean; // Can be called from browser requiresProxy?: boolean; // Needs backend proxy // ... } ``` The `requiresProxy` field is a boolean flag in the `AIProviderMetadata` interface that indicates whether a provider requires a backend proxy for browser-based access. **Provider Usage by Type:** | Provider | supportsCORS | requiresProxy | Notes | |----------|-------------|---------------|-------| | **Free Tier Providers** | | | | | OpenRouter | true | false | Supports CORS directly | | Groq | false | true | Marked for proxy but attempts direct | | HuggingFace | true | false | Supports CORS directly | | Mistral | false | true | Marked for proxy but attempts direct | | **Paid Providers** | | | | | Anthropic | true | false | **UNIQUE**: Only paid provider with CORS support! | | OpenAI | false | true | No CORS support | | Gemini | false | true | Recommended for production with proxy | | xAI (Grok) | false | true | Likely requires proxy like OpenAI | | DeepSeek | false | true | Requires backend proxy | --- ### 2. How Providers are Configured with CORS and Proxy Settings **Configuration Pattern in Provider Files:** Each provider declares its CORS/proxy support in the metadata object. Examples: **OpenAI** (requires proxy): ```typescript // src/services/ai/openaiProvider.ts (lines 33-34) supportsCORS: false, requiresProxy: true, // OpenAI does NOT support CORS ``` **Anthropic** (supports CORS with special header): ```typescript // src/services/ai/anthropicProvider.ts (lines 34-35) supportsCORS: true, // Claude API now supports CORS (Aug 2024)! requiresProxy: false, // Can call directly from browser (with special header) ``` With special initialization: ```typescript // src/services/ai/anthropicProvider.ts (lines 120-127) this.client = new Anthropic({ apiKey: apiKey, dangerouslyAllowBrowser: true, // Required for browser use defaultHeaders: { // Required header for CORS support (added Aug 2024) 'anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access': 'true', }, }); ``` **OpenRouter** (full CORS support): ```typescript // src/services/ai/openRouterProvider.ts (lines 43-44) supportsCORS: true, requiresProxy: false, ``` --- ### 3. UI That Displays "Proxy Required" to Users **Location:** `src/components/ProviderSettings.tsx` (lines 196-200) The warning badge is rendered conditionally: ```typescript {!metadata.supportsCORS && metadata.requiresProxy && ( ⚠️ Proxy Required )} ``` **Visual Behavior:** - Shows a yellow warning badge with "⚠️ Proxy Required" label - Only displays if: `!supportsCORS && requiresProxy` - Appears next to the provider name in the Provider Settings modal - Includes tooltip: "Requires backend proxy for browser use" **Provider Settings Modal Context:** The badge appears in the provider configuration list around: - Provider name and description - "Free Models" badge (if applicable) - "Configured" / "Not Configured" status - API key input field - Save/Update and Test buttons --- ### 4. Why Some Providers Need a Proxy (CORS Restrictions) **CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) Issue:** CORS is a browser security mechanism that prevents JavaScript from making requests to domains other than the one serving the page. API providers can choose to enable CORS headers in their responses to allow browser-based access. **Provider Breakdown:** **Providers WITHOUT CORS Support (need proxy):** - **OpenAI** (line 314-319 in openaiProvider.ts): Error handling shows CORS error detection: ```typescript } else if (error?.message?.includes('CORS') || error?.message?.includes('fetch')) { throw new ProviderError( ProviderErrorType.NETWORK_ERROR, 'OpenAI requires backend proxy for browser use. CORS error encountered.', 'openai' ); ``` - **xAI Grok** (line 35 in xaiProvider.ts): Comment notes it "Likely requires proxy like OpenAI" - **DeepSeek, Gemini, Groq, Mistral**: All marked with `requiresProxy: true` in their metadata **Providers WITH CORS Support (no proxy needed):** - **OpenRouter** (line 24): "Access 200+ AI models through one API. Best flexibility and model selection." - Achieves this by proxying requests to other providers on their servers - User can call OpenRouter directly from browser - **HuggingFace** (line 41): Free tier with native CORS support - **Anthropic** (lines 34-35): "Claude API now supports CORS (Aug 2024)!" - Added special header requirement: `anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access: true` - Notable: Only paid provider with CORS support - Comment: "Claude API now supports CORS (Aug 2024)!" **Architecture Decision:** The system implements a "Zero-Data-Host" privacy-first architecture where: - Direct browser-to-API calls are preferred (preserves user privacy) - No central proxy server is used (users' API keys stay on their device) - Providers that don't support CORS require users to implement their own backend proxy - OpenRouter acts as a relay for accessing non-CORS providers through one interface --- ### Key Files for Reference - **Type Definitions:** `/src/services/ai/types.ts` (lines 63-64) - **UI Component:** `/src/components/ProviderSettings.tsx` (lines 196-200) - **Provider Implementations:** `/src/services/ai/[providerName]Provider.ts` - **Documentation:** `/docs/technical/multi_provider_ai_terminal.md` (lines 119-127 for CORS explanation) - **API Integration Guide:** `/docs/technical/ai_provider_integration.md` (lines 84-85 for template) --- ### Summary "Proxy Required" indicates that a specific AI provider doesn't allow direct browser-to-API calls due to CORS restrictions. These providers (OpenAI, xAI, Gemini, etc.) block cross-origin requests for security reasons. Users who want to use these providers from the browser have two options: 1. **Use an intermediary service** like OpenRouter (which proxies requests) 2. **Implement their own backend proxy** to forward requests server-to-server Anthropic is unique as the only paid provider offering CORS support, making Claude accessible directly from the browser with a special header flag. This design preserves user privacy by avoiding a central proxy infrastructure.

Where `requiresProxy` is Defined and Used **Definition Location:** `src/services/ai/types.ts` (lines 63-64) ```typescript export interface AIProviderMetadata { // ... supportsCORS?: boolean; // Can be called from browser requiresProxy?: boolean; // Needs backend proxy // ... } ``` The `requiresProxy` field is a boolean flag in the `AIProviderMetadata` interface that indicates whether a provider requires a backend proxy for browser-based access. **Provider Usage by Type:** | Provider | supportsCORS | requiresProxy | Notes | |----------|-------------|---------------|-------| | **Free Tier Providers** | | | | | OpenRouter | true | false | Supports CORS directly | | Groq | false | true | Marked for proxy but attempts direct | | HuggingFace | true | false | Supports CORS directly | | Mistral | false | true | Marked for proxy but attempts direct | | **Paid Providers** | | | | | Anthropic | true | false | **UNIQUE**: Only paid provider with CORS support! | | OpenAI | false | true | No CORS support | | Gemini | false | true | Recommended for production with proxy | | xAI (Grok) | false | true | Likely requires proxy like OpenAI | | DeepSeek | false | true | Requires backend proxy | --- ### 2. How Providers are Configured with CORS and Proxy Settings **Configuration Pattern in Provider Files:** Each provider declares its CORS/proxy support in the metadata object. Examples: **OpenAI** (requires proxy): ```typescript // src/services/ai/openaiProvider.ts (lines 33-34) supportsCORS: false, requiresProxy: true, // OpenAI does NOT support CORS ``` **Anthropic** (supports CORS with special header): ```typescript // src/services/ai/anthropicProvider.ts (lines 34-35) supportsCORS: true, // Claude API now supports CORS (Aug 2024)! requiresProxy: false, // Can call directly from browser (with special header) ``` With special initialization: ```typescript // src/services/ai/anthropicProvider.ts (lines 120-127) this.client = new Anthropic({ apiKey: apiKey, dangerouslyAllowBrowser: true, // Required for browser use defaultHeaders: { // Required header for CORS support (added Aug 2024) 'anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access': 'true', }, }); ``` **OpenRouter** (full CORS support): ```typescript // src/services/ai/openRouterProvider.ts (lines 43-44) supportsCORS: true, requiresProxy: false, ``` --- ### 3. UI That Displays "Proxy Required" to Users **Location:** `src/components/ProviderSettings.tsx` (lines 196-200) The warning badge is rendered conditionally: ```typescript {!metadata.supportsCORS && metadata.requiresProxy && ( ⚠️ Proxy Required )} ``` **Visual Behavior:** - Shows a yellow warning badge with "⚠️ Proxy Required" label - Only displays if: `!supportsCORS && requiresProxy` - Appears next to the provider name in the Provider Settings modal - Includes tooltip: "Requires backend proxy for browser use" **Provider Settings Modal Context:** The badge appears in the provider configuration list around: - Provider name and description - "Free Models" badge (if applicable) - "Configured" / "Not Configured" status - API key input field - Save/Update and Test buttons --- ### 4. Why Some Providers Need a Proxy (CORS Restrictions) **CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) Issue:** CORS is a browser security mechanism that prevents JavaScript from making requests to domains other than the one serving the page. API providers can choose to enable CORS headers in their responses to allow browser-based access. **Provider Breakdown:** **Providers WITHOUT CORS Support (need proxy):** - **OpenAI** (line 314-319 in openaiProvider.ts): Error handling shows CORS error detection: ```typescript } else if (error?.message?.includes('CORS') || error?.message?.includes('fetch')) { throw new ProviderError( ProviderErrorType.NETWORK_ERROR, 'OpenAI requires backend proxy for browser use. CORS error encountered.', 'openai' ); ``` - **xAI Grok** (line 35 in xaiProvider.ts): Comment notes it "Likely requires proxy like OpenAI" - **DeepSeek, Gemini, Groq, Mistral**: All marked with `requiresProxy: true` in their metadata **Providers WITH CORS Support (no proxy needed):** - **OpenRouter** (line 24): "Access 200+ AI models through one API. Best flexibility and model selection." - Achieves this by proxying requests to other providers on their servers - User can call OpenRouter directly from browser - **HuggingFace** (line 41): Free tier with native CORS support - **Anthropic** (lines 34-35): "Claude API now supports CORS (Aug 2024)!" - Added special header requirement: `anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access: true` - Notable: Only paid provider with CORS support - Comment: "Claude API now supports CORS (Aug 2024)!" **Architecture Decision:** The system implements a "Zero-Data-Host" privacy-first architecture where: - Direct browser-to-API calls are preferred (preserves user privacy) - No central proxy server is used (users' API keys stay on their device) - Providers that don't support CORS require users to implement their own backend proxy - OpenRouter acts as a relay for accessing non-CORS providers through one interface --- ### Key Files for Reference - **Type Definitions:** `/src/services/ai/types.ts` (lines 63-64) - **UI Component:** `/src/components/ProviderSettings.tsx` (lines 196-200) - **Provider Implementations:** `/src/services/ai/[providerName]Provider.ts` - **Documentation:** `/docs/technical/multi_provider_ai_terminal.md` (lines 119-127 for CORS explanation) - **API Integration Guide:** `/docs/technical/ai_provider_integration.md` (lines 84-85 for template) --- ### Summary "Proxy Required" indicates that a specific AI provider doesn't allow direct browser-to-API calls due to CORS restrictions. These providers (OpenAI, xAI, Gemini, etc.) block cross-origin requests for security reasons. Users who want to use these providers from the browser have two options: 1. **Use an intermediary service** like OpenRouter (which proxies requests) 2. **Implement their own backend proxy** to forward requests server-to-server Anthropic is unique as the only paid provider offering CORS support, making Claude accessible directly from the browser with a special header flag. This design preserves user privacy by avoiding a central proxy infrastructure.

Great, I now have comprehensive information from all three exploration agents. Let me synthesize this and answer the user's questions:

1. **"Proxy Required" meaning**: This indicates that a provider doesn't support CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing). Because Neuman Brain is a browser-based app with a "Zero-Data-Host" privacy architecture, it tries to call AI APIs directly from the browser. Providers like OpenAI, xAI, Gemini, etc. block these cross-origin requests for security. Users would need to either:
   - Use OpenRouter (which proxies requests)
   - Set up their own backend proxy

2. **Ping error**: The `ping` command requires WebContainer to be booted first. WebContainer is a Node.js runtime in the browser using WebAssembly. It takes 2-5 seconds to boot. The user needs to run `/version` first to trigger the boot, then commands will work. However, `ping` specifically may NOT work because WebContainer only supports Node.js commands (npm, node, npx, ls, cd, etc.) - not system-level networking commands like ping.

3. **Documentation**: There's already an AI Terminal guide at `docs/user/ai_terminal_guide.md` and a Phantom Shell guide at `docs/product/phantom-shell-guide.md`. The user wants to improve/expand these.

Let me ask clarifying questions and then create a plan.

content.text:
Excellent findings! Let me summarize what I learned and clarify a few things:

## Answers to Your Questions

### 1. "Proxy Required" Meaning
This indicates **CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) restrictions**. Since Neuman Brain is a privacy-first browser app that calls AI APIs directly (no central server), some providers block these requests:

| Provider | CORS Support | Notes |
|----------|-------------|-------|
| OpenRouter | ✅ Yes | Acts as relay - best flexibility |
| HuggingFace | ✅ Yes | Free tier works directly |
| Anthropic | ✅ Yes | Added Aug 2024 with special header |
| OpenAI | ❌ No | Needs proxy |
| xAI/Grok | ❌ No | Needs proxy |
| DeepSeek | ❌ No | Needs proxy |
| Gemini | ❌ No | Needs proxy |

**Workaround:** Use OpenRouter to access non-CORS providers through one API.

### 2. The `ping` Error
Two issues here:
1. **WebContainer needs to boot first** (2-5 seconds) - run `/version` to trigger it
2. **`ping` won't work anyway** - WebContainer only supports Node.js commands (`npm`, `node`, `npx`, `ls`, `cd`, etc.), NOT system networking commands like `ping`, `curl`, `git`, or `docker`

### 3. Existing Documentation
Good news - documentation already exists:
- `docs/user/ai_terminal_guide.md` (945 lines - covers AI chat mode)
- `docs/product/phantom-shell-guide.md` (191 lines - covers shell mode)

However, these could be improved/consolidated.

# Plan: Terminal & AI Documentation Overhaul

## Overview
Create comprehensive, user-friendly documentation for the AI Terminal and Phantom Shell that serves users from beginners to advanced developers. Includes unified docs, in-app help, and backend proxy guide.

## Deliverables

### 1. Unified User Guide
**File:** `docs/user/terminal-complete-guide.md`

**Structure:**
```
# Neuman Brain Terminal - Complete Guide

## Quick Start (Beginners)
- What is the terminal?
- Two modes: AI Chat vs Phantom Shell
- Your first AI conversation
- Your first shell command

## AI Chat Mode
- Supported providers (with CORS status table)
- Setting up API keys (step-by-step with screenshots)
- Provider recommendations by use case
- Understanding "Proxy Required" warning
- Troubleshooting common errors

## Phantom Shell Mode
- What is WebContainer?
- Available commands (built-in vs system)
- Commands that DON'T work (ping, git, docker, python)
- Project management
- AI-assisted commands (/ai)
- Live preview pane

## Advanced: Backend Proxy Setup
- Why you might need a proxy
- Option 1: Use OpenRouter (easiest)
- Option 2: Cloudflare Worker proxy
- Option 3: Self-hosted Node.js proxy
- Security considerations

## Keyboard Shortcuts Reference
## Troubleshooting FAQ
## Glossary (CORS, WebContainer, etc.)
```

### 2. In-App Help System
**Files to modify:**
- `src/components/AITerminal.tsx` - Add help button and modal
- `src/components/TerminalHelpModal.tsx` (NEW) - Comprehensive help modal

**Features:**
- Help button (?) in terminal header
- Tabbed modal: Quick Start | AI Providers | Shell Commands | Troubleshooting
- Contextual tooltips on "Proxy Required" badges
- First-time onboarding for new users
- `/help` command improvements in Phantom Shell

### 3. Update Existing Docs
**Files to update:**
- `docs/user/ai_terminal_guide.md` - Add CORS/proxy explanation
- `docs/product/phantom-shell-guide.md` - Add WebContainer limitations clearly

### 4. Provider Settings Enhancement
**File:** `src/components/ProviderSettings.tsx`

**Changes:**
- Add tooltip explaining "Proxy Required" with link to docs
- Add "Recommended for beginners" badge on OpenRouter/Anthropic
- Show which providers work without proxy

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Documentation Files
1. Create `docs/user/terminal-complete-guide.md` (unified guide)
2. Create `docs/user/backend-proxy-setup.md` (advanced proxy guide)
3. Update `docs/user/ai_terminal_guide.md` with CORS section
4. Update `docs/product/phantom-shell-guide.md` with limitations

### Phase 2: In-App Help Modal
1. Create `src/components/TerminalHelpModal.tsx`
2. Add help button to AITerminal header
3. Implement tabbed interface with:
   - Quick Start tab
   - AI Providers tab (with CORS table)
   - Shell Commands tab
   - Troubleshooting tab

### Phase 3: UI Enhancements
1. Add tooltip to "Proxy Required" badge in ProviderSettings
2. Add "Beginner Friendly" badges to OpenRouter/HuggingFace/Anthropic
3. Improve `/help` command output in Phantom Shell
4. Add first-time user detection and onboarding prompt

## Files to Create
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `docs/user/terminal-complete-guide.md` | Unified comprehensive guide |
| `docs/user/backend-proxy-setup.md` | Advanced proxy configuration |
| `src/components/TerminalHelpModal.tsx` | In-app help modal component |

## Files to Modify
| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `src/components/AITerminal.tsx` | Add help button, integrate modal |
| `src/components/ProviderSettings.tsx` | Add tooltips, beginner badges |
| `src/services/phantomShell/shellService.ts` | Improve /help output |
| `docs/user/ai_terminal_guide.md` | Add CORS/proxy section |
| `docs/product/phantom-shell-guide.md` | Add clear limitations section |

## Content Highlights

### CORS Explanation (User-Friendly)
```markdown
## Why Some Providers Show "Proxy Required"

Neuman Brain runs entirely in your browser for privacy - your API keys
never touch our servers. However, some AI providers (OpenAI, xAI, etc.)
block direct browser requests for security reasons.

**Easy Solution:** Use OpenRouter - it works directly and can access
200+ models including GPT-4, Claude, and more through one API key.

**For Power Users:** Set up your own proxy server (see Advanced Guide).
```

### WebContainer Limitations (Clear)
```markdown
## What Works vs What Doesn't

✅ WORKS: npm, node, npx, ls, cd, cat, mkdir, basic shell
❌ DOESN'T WORK: git, docker, python, ping, curl, system commands

WebContainer runs Node.js in your browser - it's not a full Linux system.
For git, use the GitHub widget or external tools.
```

## Estimated Effort
- Phase 1 (Docs): ~2 hours
- Phase 2 (Help Modal): ~1.5 hours
- Phase 3 (UI Polish): ~1 hour
- **Total: ~4.5 hours**

## Success Criteria
- New user can start AI chat within 2 minutes
- "Proxy Required" meaning is clear without reading docs
- WebContainer limitations are obvious before user hits errors
- Advanced users have clear path to set up their own proxy

input.content:
# Neuman Brain Terminal - Complete Guide

> Your AI-powered terminal for conversations and development, running entirely in your browser.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
2. [AI Chat Mode](#ai-chat-mode)
3. [Phantom Shell Mode](#phantom-shell-mode)
4. [Keyboard Shortcuts](#keyboard-shortcuts)
5. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
6. [Glossary](#glossary)

---

## Quick Start

### What is the Terminal?

The Neuman Brain Terminal is a versatile tool with **two modes**:

| Mode | Purpose | Best For |
|------|---------|----------|
| **AI Chat** | Converse with AI models | Questions, brainstorming, coding help |
| **Phantom Shell** | Run development commands | npm, node, building projects |

### Opening the Terminal

- **Keyboard:** Press `Ctrl+Shift+A` (Windows/Linux) or `Cmd+Shift+A` (Mac)
- **Click:** The terminal icon in the bottom panel

### Your First AI Conversation

1. Open the terminal (it starts in AI Chat mode)
2. Type your question and press Enter
3. Watch the AI respond in real-time

```
You: What's the best way to center a div in CSS?

AI: There are several modern approaches to center a div:

1. **Flexbox (recommended)**
   .container {
     display: flex;
     justify-content: center;
     align-items: center;
   }
...
```

### Your First Shell Command

1. Click the **Shell** tab or type `/shell` in AI Chat
2. Type `/version` to boot WebContainer
3. Try `node -v` to see Node.js version

```
phantom:~$ /version
Phantom Shell v1.0.0
WebContainer: Ready ✓
Node.js: v18.x

phantom:~$ node -v
v18.20.2
```

---

## AI Chat Mode

### Supported Providers

Neuman Brain supports **9 AI providers**. Some work directly in your browser, others require a proxy.

| Provider | CORS Support | Free Tier | Recommendation |
|----------|-------------|-----------|----------------|
| **OpenRouter** | ✅ Direct | ✅ Yes | Best for beginners - access 200+ models |
| **HuggingFace** | ✅ Direct | ✅ Yes | Great free option |
| **Anthropic** | ✅ Direct | ❌ No | Best quality (Claude) |
| **Groq** | ⚠️ Limited | ✅ Yes | Fastest responses |
| **Mistral** | ⚠️ Limited | ✅ Yes | European option |
| **Gemini** | ❌ Proxy | ✅ Yes | Google's models |
| **OpenAI** | ❌ Proxy | ❌ No | GPT-4, ChatGPT |
| **xAI** | ❌ Proxy | ❌ No | Grok models |
| **DeepSeek** | ❌ Proxy | ❌ No | Excellent value |

### Understanding "Proxy Required"

You may see a yellow **⚠️ Proxy Required** badge next to some providers. Here's what it means:

**Why does this happen?**

Neuman Brain runs entirely in your browser for privacy - your API keys never touch our servers. However, some AI providers (OpenAI, xAI, Gemini, DeepSeek) block direct browser requests due to CORS security policies.

**Easy Solutions:**

1. **Use OpenRouter** (Recommended)
   - Works directly in browser
   - Access 200+ models including GPT-4, Claude, Llama
   - One API key for everything
   - Get key at: [openrouter.ai/keys](https://openrouter.ai/keys)

2. **Use Anthropic** (For Claude)
   - Direct browser access (added August 2024)
   - Best quality responses
   - Get key at: [console.anthropic.com](https://console.anthropic.com)

3. **Set up your own proxy** (Advanced)
   - See [Backend Proxy Setup Guide](./backend-proxy-setup.md)

### Setting Up API Keys

1. Click the **gear icon** (⚙️) in the terminal header
2. Find your preferred provider
3. Click **Add API Key**
4. Paste your key and click **Save**
5. Click **Test** to verify it works

**Where to get API keys:**

| Provider | URL | Free Credits |
|----------|-----|--------------|
| OpenRouter | [openrouter.ai/keys](https://openrouter.ai/keys) | $1 free |
| Anthropic | [console.anthropic.com](https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys) | None |
| OpenAI | [platform.openai.com/api-keys](https://platform.openai.com/api-keys) | $5 free (new accounts) |
| Groq | [console.groq.com/keys](https://console.groq.com/keys) | Generous free tier |
| HuggingFace | [huggingface.co/settings/tokens](https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens) | Free |
| DeepSeek | [platform.deepseek.com/api_keys](https://platform.deepseek.com/api_keys) | ~$5 free |
| Gemini | [aistudio.google.com](https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey) | Free tier |

### Choosing the Right Model

**For General Chat:**
- OpenRouter → `meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct` (free, fast)
- Anthropic → `claude-3-5-sonnet` (best quality)

**For Coding:**
- DeepSeek → `deepseek-coder` (excellent, cheap)
- Anthropic → `claude-3-5-sonnet` (best overall)

**For Fast Responses:**
- Groq → `llama-3.3-70b-versatile` (lightning fast)

**For Complex Reasoning:**
- DeepSeek → `deepseek-reasoner` (R1 model)
- OpenAI → `o1` (via OpenRouter)

### Privacy & Security

Your API keys are:
- ✅ Encrypted with AES-256-GCM before storage
- ✅ Stored only in your browser's IndexedDB
- ✅ Never sent to Neuman Brain servers
- ✅ Only sent directly to the AI provider you choose

---

## Phantom Shell Mode

### What is Phantom Shell?

Phantom Shell is a **real development terminal** running in your browser. It uses **WebContainer** technology to run Node.js directly in the browser - no installation needed.

### What is WebContainer?

WebContainer is Node.js compiled to WebAssembly. It provides:
- Real npm/node execution
- In-memory file system
- Process spawning
- Dev server support

**First-time boot takes 2-5 seconds.** After that, it's instant.

### What Works vs What Doesn't

| ✅ WORKS | ❌ DOESN'T WORK |
|----------|----------------|
| `npm install` | `git clone` |
| `npm run dev` | `docker` |
| `node script.js` | `python` |
| `npx create-react-app` | `ping` |
| `ls`, `cd`, `cat`, `mkdir` | `curl`, `wget` |

**Why?** WebContainer runs Node.js in the browser - it's not a full Linux system. For git, use the GitHub widget or external tools.

### Built-in Commands

Type `/help` to see all commands:

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/help` | Show all commands |
| `/version` | Boot WebContainer, show status |
| `/clear` | Clear terminal screen |
| `/history` | Show command history |
| `/projects` | List your projects |
| `/new <name>` | Create new project |
| `/open <name>` | Open a project |
| `/close` | Close current project |
| `/ai <prompt>` | Ask AI for help |

### System Commands

After WebContainer boots, run standard Node.js commands:

```bash
# Check versions
node -v
npm -v

# Create a new project
npm init -y
npm install express

# Run scripts
node index.js
npm run dev
npm run build

# File operations
ls -la
cd src
cat package.json
mkdir components
```

### AI-Assisted Commands

Ask the AI to help with commands:

```bash
/ai create a react app with typescript

# AI responds with:
# npx create-react-app my-app --template typescript
# cd my-app
# npm start
```

### Live Preview

When you run a dev server (`npm run dev`), the preview pane automatically shows your app:

1. Run `npm run dev` in a project
2. Preview pane opens on the right
3. See changes in real-time

Toggle preview with the split button in the terminal header.

### Projects

Phantom Shell supports multiple projects:

```bash
/new my-react-app    # Create project
/open my-react-app   # Open it
/projects            # List all projects
/close               # Close current project
```

Projects are saved to IndexedDB and persist across sessions.

---

## Keyboard Shortcuts

### Global

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| `Ctrl/Cmd + Shift + A` | Toggle terminal |
| `Escape` | Close terminal |

### Terminal

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| `Enter` | Send message / Run command |
| `Up/Down` | Navigate command history |
| `Ctrl + C` | Cancel current input |
| `Ctrl + L` | Clear screen |
| `Ctrl + Shift + F` | Search in terminal |

### AI Chat

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| `Tab` | Switch provider/model |
| `Ctrl + N` | New conversation |

---

## Troubleshooting

### "Proxy Required" - Can't use OpenAI/xAI/DeepSeek

**Problem:** These providers don't allow direct browser requests.

**Solutions:**
1. Use **OpenRouter** instead - it proxies requests for you
2. Use **Anthropic** for Claude (supports direct browser access)
3. Set up your own proxy server ([guide](./backend-proxy-setup.md))

### "WebContainer not ready"

**Problem:** You tried to run a system command before WebContainer booted.

**Solution:** Run `/version` first to boot WebContainer (takes 2-5 seconds).

### "ping/git/docker doesn't work"

**Problem:** WebContainer only supports Node.js commands.

**What works:** npm, node, npx, ls, cd, cat, mkdir
**What doesn't:** ping, git, docker, python, curl, system commands

**Alternatives:**
- For git: Use the GitHub widget or external git client
- For testing connectivity: Use `node -e "fetch('https://example.com')"`

### "API key invalid" error

**Solutions:**
1. Check the key is copied correctly (no extra spaces)
2. Verify the key at the provider's website
3. Check if key has expired or been revoked
4. Ensure you have credits/quota remaining

### "Rate limit exceeded"

**Problem:** You've hit the provider's request limit.

**Solutions:**
1. Wait a few minutes and try again
2. Switch to a different provider temporarily
3. Upgrade your API plan for higher limits

### Terminal is slow or unresponsive

**Solutions:**
1. Run `/clear` to clear the buffer
2. Refresh the page
3. Check browser console for errors
4. Ensure you're using a supported browser (Chrome 89+, Firefox 89+, Safari 16.4+)

---

## Glossary

### CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing)
A browser security feature that restricts which websites can make requests to other domains. Some AI providers don't allow CORS, which is why they show "Proxy Required."

### WebContainer
A browser-based Node.js runtime using WebAssembly. It allows running real npm/node commands without installing anything locally.

### API Key
A secret string that authenticates you with an AI provider. Keep it private - anyone with your key can use your account.

### Streaming
Real-time delivery of AI responses, so you see text appear as it's generated rather than waiting for the complete response.

### Provider
A company offering AI models through an API (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.).

### Model
A specific AI system within a provider (GPT-4, Claude 3.5, Llama 3.3, etc.).

### Token
The basic unit AI models use to process text. Roughly 4 characters = 1 token.

### Context Window
How much text an AI model can "remember" in a conversation. Larger = can handle longer conversations.

---

## Getting Help

- **In-app:** Press `?` or click the help icon
- **Documentation:** [docs/user/](./README.md)
- **Issues:** [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues)

---

*Last updated: November 2025 | Version 2.0*

content.content: File created successfully at: D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase\docs\user\terminal-complete-guide.md

## Phantom Shell Mode

### What is Phantom Shell?

Phantom Shell is a **real development terminal** running in your browser. It uses **WebContainer** technology to run Node.js directly in the browser - no installation needed.

### What is WebContainer?

WebContainer is Node.js compiled to WebAssembly. It provides:
- Real npm/node execution
- In-memory file system
- Process spawning
- Dev server support

**First-time boot takes 2-5 seconds.** After that, it's instant.

### What Works vs What Doesn't

| ✅ WORKS | ❌ DOESN'T WORK |
|----------|----------------|
| `npm install` | `git clone` |
| `npm run dev` | `docker` |
| `node script.js` | `python` |
| `npx create-react-app` | `ping` |
| `ls`, `cd`, `cat`, `mkdir` | `curl`, `wget` |

**Why?** WebContainer runs Node.js in the browser - it's not a full Linux system. For git, use the GitHub widget or external tools.

### Built-in Commands

Type `/help` to see all commands:

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/help` | Show all commands |
| `/version` | Boot WebContainer, show status |
| `/clear` | Clear terminal screen |
| `/history` | Show command history |
| `/projects` | List your projects |
| `/new <name>` | Create new project |
| `/open <name>` | Open a project |
| `/close` | Close current project |
| `/ai <prompt>` | Ask AI for help |

### System Commands

After WebContainer boots, run standard Node.js commands:

```bash
# Check versions
node -v
npm -v

# Create a new project
npm init -y
npm install express

# Run scripts
node index.js
npm run dev
npm run build

# File operations
ls -la
cd src
cat package.json
mkdir components
```

### AI-Assisted Commands

Ask the AI to help with commands:

```bash
/ai create a react app with typescript

# AI responds with:
# npx create-react-app my-app --template typescript
# cd my-app
# npm start
```

### Live Preview

When you run a dev server (`npm run dev`), the preview pane automatically shows your app:

1. Run `npm run dev` in a project
2. Preview pane opens on the right
3. See changes in real-time

Toggle preview with the split button in the terminal header.

### Projects

Phantom Shell supports multiple projects:

```bash
/new my-react-app    # Create project
/open my-react-app   # Open it
/projects            # List all projects
/close               # Close current project
```

Projects are saved to IndexedDB and persist across sessions.

---

## Keyboard Shortcuts

### Global

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| `Ctrl/Cmd + Shift + A` | Toggle terminal |
| `Escape` | Close terminal |

### Terminal

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| `Enter` | Send message / Run command |
| `Up/Down` | Navigate command history |
| `Ctrl + C` | Cancel current input |
| `Ctrl + L` | Clear screen |
| `Ctrl + Shift + F` | Search in terminal |

### AI Chat

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| `Tab` | Switch provider/model |
| `Ctrl + N` | New conversation |

---

## Troubleshooting

### "Proxy Required" - Can't use OpenAI/xAI/DeepSeek

**Problem:** These providers don't allow direct browser requests.

**Solutions:**
1. Use **OpenRouter** instead - it proxies requests for you
2. Use **Anthropic** for Claude (supports direct browser access)
3. Set up your own proxy server ([guide](./backend-proxy-setup.md))

### "WebContainer not ready"

**Problem:** You tried to run a system command before WebContainer booted.

**Solution:** Run `/version` first to boot WebContainer (takes 2-5 seconds).

### "ping/git/docker doesn't work"

**Problem:** WebContainer only supports Node.js commands.

**What works:** npm, node, npx, ls, cd, cat, mkdir
**What doesn't:** ping, git, docker, python, curl, system commands

**Alternatives:**
- For git: Use the GitHub widget or external git client
- For testing connectivity: Use `node -e "fetch('https://example.com')"`

### "API key invalid" error

**Solutions:**
1. Check the key is copied correctly (no extra spaces)
2. Verify the key at the provider's website
3. Check if key has expired or been revoked
4. Ensure you have credits/quota remaining

### "Rate limit exceeded"

**Problem:** You've hit the provider's request limit.

**Solutions:**
1. Wait a few minutes and try again
2. Switch to a different provider temporarily
3. Upgrade your API plan for higher limits

### Terminal is slow or unresponsive

**Solutions:**
1. Run `/clear` to clear the buffer
2. Refresh the page
3. Check browser console for errors
4. Ensure you're using a supported browser (Chrome 89+, Firefox 89+, Safari 16.4+)

---

## Glossary

### CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing)
A browser security feature that restricts which websites can make requests to other domains. Some AI providers don't allow CORS, which is why they show "Proxy Required."

### WebContainer
A browser-based Node.js runtime using WebAssembly. It allows running real npm/node commands without installing anything locally.

### API Key
A secret string that authenticates you with an AI provider. Keep it private - anyone with your key can use your account.

### Streaming
Real-time delivery of AI responses, so you see text appear as it's generated rather than waiting for the complete response.

### Provider
A company offering AI models through an API (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.).

### Model
A specific AI system within a provider (GPT-4, Claude 3.5, Llama 3.3, etc.).

### Token
The basic unit AI models use to process text. Roughly 4 characters = 1 token.

### Context Window
How much text an AI model can "remember" in a conversation. Larger = can handle longer conversations.

---

## Getting Help

- **In-app:** Press `?` or click the help icon
- **Documentation:** [docs/user/](./README.md)
- **Issues:** [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues)

---

*Last updated: November 2025 | Version 2.0*
