file.content:
# Phantom Shell User Guide

**Browser-Native Development Environment**

---

## What is Phantom Shell?

Phantom Shell is a **browser-native terminal** that runs directly in your browser. It provides a real development environment without installing anything on your computer.

**Key Features:**
- Full terminal emulator (not a chat interface)
- Runs Node.js and npm commands in your browser via WebContainers
- Command history with up/down arrow navigation
- Project persistence (saved to IndexedDB)
- Works entirely offline after initial load
- Zero data sent to servers - privacy-first

---

## Accessing Phantom Shell

1. Click the **AI Terminal** button (💬) in the header or press `Ctrl+Shift+A` / `Cmd+Shift+A`
2. You'll see two tabs at the top: **💬 Chat** and **⌨️ Shell**
3. Click **⌨️ Shell** to switch to Phantom Shell mode

---

## Built-in Commands

Phantom Shell supports several built-in commands (prefix with `/`):

### Help & Info

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/help` | Show all available commands |
| `/version` | Show version info and WebContainer status |
| `/about` | About Phantom Shell |
| `/clear` | Clear the terminal screen |
| `/history` | Show your recent command history |

### Project Management

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/projects` | List all your projects |
| `/new <name>` | Create a new project |
| `/open <name>` | Open an existing project |
| `/close` | Close the current project |
| `/export` | Export project to .brain file (coming soon) |

### AI Commands (Coming Soon)

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/ai <prompt>` | Ask AI to help with development |

---

## Keyboard Shortcuts

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| `Enter` | Execute command |
| `↑` / `↓` | Navigate command history |
| `Ctrl+C` | Cancel current input |
| `Backspace` | Delete last character |

---

## System Commands (WebContainer)

Once WebContainer boots, you can run standard Node.js commands:

```bash
# Check Node.js version
node -v

# Initialize a project
npm init -y

# Install packages
npm install lodash

# Run Node.js scripts
node index.js

# Run npm scripts
npm run dev
```

**Note:** WebContainer requires Cross-Origin Isolation headers. You'll see a ✓ or ✗ status in `/version` output.

---

## Browser Requirements

Phantom Shell uses WebContainers which requires modern browser features:

| Browser | Support |
|---------|---------|
| Chrome 89+ | ✅ Full support |
| Edge 89+ | ✅ Full support |
| Firefox 89+ | ✅ Full support |
| Safari 16.4+ | ✅ Full support |

**Technical Requirements:**
- SharedArrayBuffer support
- Cross-Origin Isolation (COEP/COOP headers)

---

## Project Templates

When creating a new project with `/new`, you can use templates:

### React + Vite (`react-vite`)
- React 18 with Vite bundler
- Hot module replacement (HMR)
- Ready to run `npm run dev`

### Node.js Basic (`node-basic`)
- Minimal Node.js project
- ES Modules enabled
- Ready for server-side development

---

## Data Storage

All your Phantom Shell data is stored locally:

- **Projects:** Saved to IndexedDB (persists across sessions)
- **Command History:** Saved locally (last 1000 commands)
- **Files:** Stored in browser memory during session, persisted to IndexedDB

**Privacy:** No data is sent to any server. Everything runs locally in your browser.

---

## Troubleshooting

### WebContainer Not Available

If you see "WebContainer requires Cross-Origin Isolation":

1. Make sure you're using a supported browser
2. Check that you're accessing via `https://` or `localhost`
3. Verify headers are configured (for self-hosted deployments)

### Commands Not Executing

1. Wait for WebContainer to finish booting (2-5 seconds)
2. Check `/version` for WebContainer status
3. Try `/help` to verify built-in commands work

### Terminal Not Responding

1. Try pressing `Ctrl+C` to cancel
2. Click the **💬 Chat** tab and back to **⌨️ Shell**
3. Refresh the page if needed (your projects are saved)

---

## Coming Soon

- **Phase 3:** IndexedDB persistence verification
- **Phase 4:** AI-assisted development (`/ai create react app`)
- **Phase 5:** Live preview pane for dev servers
- **Phase 6:** Git integration via isomorphic-git

---

## Technical Details

Phantom Shell is built with:

- **xterm.js** - Full-featured terminal emulator
- **WebContainers** - Node.js runtime in browser (WebAssembly)
- **Zustand** - State management with persistence
- **IndexedDB** - Local project storage

**Bundle Impact:** ~200KB (lazy loaded when you open the terminal)

---

**Related Docs:**
- [AI Terminal Guide](./ai_terminal_guide.md) - Chat with AI providers
- [Feature Map](./feature_map.md) - Full feature inventory

1→# AI Terminal User Guide
     2→
     3→**Welcome to the Multi-Provider AI Terminal!**
     4→
     5→Get help with coding, questions, productivity, and more using 8 different AI providers with automatic fallback and encrypted key storage.
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Quick Start
    10→
    11→### Step 1: Open the AI Terminal
    12→
    13→Click the **🤖 AI Terminal** button in the bottom-right corner of the screen.
    14→
    15→### Step 2: Configure a Provider
    16→
    17→1. Click the **⚙️ Settings** button in the terminal header
    18→2. Choose a provider (see recommendations below)
    19→3. Get an API key from the provider's website
    20→4. Enter your API key and click **Save**
    21→5. Click **Test Key** to verify it works
    22→6. Click **Done** to close settings
    23→
    24→### Step 3: Start Chatting!
    25→
    26→Type your question or request and press Enter. The AI will respond in real-time!
    27→
    28→---
    29→
    30→## Choosing a Provider
    31→
    32→### Cost Comparison Table
    33→
    34→| Provider | Free Tier | Paid Pricing | Credit Card Required | Best For |
    35→|----------|-----------|--------------|---------------------|----------|
    36→| **OpenRouter** | ✅ Many free models | $0.06-$30/1M tokens | ❌ No | Most versatile |
    37→| **Groq** | ✅ Generous free tier | N/A (free only) | ❌ No | Fastest speed |
    38→| **HuggingFace** | ✅ True free tier | N/A (free only) | ❌ No | Open source |
    39→| **Mistral** | ⚠️ Limited free | $0.25-$2/1M tokens | ⚠️ Yes | GDPR compliance |
    40→| **Gemini** | ✅ Free tier | $0.075-$1.25/1M tokens | ⚠️ Yes | Large context |
    41→| **OpenAI** | ❌ No | $0.15-$15/1M tokens | ✅ Yes | GPT-4o, o1 |
    42→| **Anthropic** | ❌ No | $1-$15/1M tokens | ✅ Yes | Best quality |
    43→| **xAI** | ❌ No | ~$5/1M tokens | ✅ Yes | Real-time info |
    44→
    45→**💡 Tip:** Start with OpenRouter or Groq (both free, no credit card needed)
    46→
    47→---
    48→
    49→## Getting API Keys: Step-by-Step
    50→
    51→### 🆓 Free Providers (Recommended to Start)
    52→
    53→#### OpenRouter
    54→
    55→**Why Choose:** Access to 200+ models (including GPT-4o, Claude, Llama) through one API. Many free models available.
    56→
    57→**Step-by-Step:**
    58→1. Go to [openrouter.ai](https://openrouter.ai)
    59→2. Click **Sign In** (top right) → Sign in with Google/GitHub/Email
    60→3. Once logged in, go to [openrouter.ai/keys](https://openrouter.ai/keys)
    61→4. Click **Create Key** button
    62→5. Give it a name (e.g., "Neuman Brain Dashboard")
    63→6. Copy the API key (starts with `sk-or-v1-...`)
    64→7. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings
    65→
    66→**Free Models Available:**
    67→- Llama 3.3 70B Instruct (quality + free)
    68→- Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking (Google's latest, free)
    69→- Mistral 7B (fast, free)
    70→- Qwen 2.5 72B (multilingual, free)
    71→
    72→**Rate Limits:** 200 requests/minute (generous!)
    73→
    74→---
    75→
    76→#### Groq
    77→
    78→**Why Choose:** Fastest AI inference in the world (custom LPU hardware). 100% free.
    79→
    80→**Step-by-Step:**
    81→1. Go to [console.groq.com](https://console.groq.com)
    82→2. Click **Sign Up** → Sign up with Google/Email
    83→3. Verify your email (check inbox)
    84→4. Once logged in, click **API Keys** in left sidebar
    85→5. Click **Create API Key** button
    86→6. Give it a name (e.g., "Neuman Brain")
    87→7. Copy the API key (starts with `gsk_...`)
    88→8. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings
    89→
    90→**Free Models Available:**
    91→- Llama 3.3 70B (Meta's latest, free)
    92→- Llama 3.1 8B (fast, free)
    93→- Mixtral 8x7B (Mistral's MoE, free)
    94→- Gemma 2 9B (Google, free)
    95→
    96→**Rate Limits:** 30 requests/minute, 14,400 requests/day (very generous!)
    97→
    98→**Speed:** Responses in <1 second (seriously!)
    99→
   100→---
   101→
   102→#### HuggingFace
   103→
   104→**Why Choose:** Thousands of open-source models. True free tier with no credit card required.
   105→
   106→**Step-by-Step:**
   107→1. Go to [huggingface.co/join](https://huggingface.co/join)
   108→2. Sign up with Email
   109→3. Verify your email (check inbox)
   110→4. Once logged in, go to [huggingface.co/settings/tokens](https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens)
   111→5. Click **New token** button
   112→6. Give it a name (e.g., "Neuman Brain Dashboard")
   113→7. Select **Read** permission (sufficient for inference)
   114→8. Click **Generate token**
   115→9. Copy the token (starts with `hf_...`)
   116→10. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings
   117→
   118→**Popular Models:**
   119→- Meta Llama models (various sizes)
   120→- Mistral models
   121→- Falcon models
   122→- Many community fine-tunes
   123→
   124→**Rate Limits:** Varies by model, generally 1,000+ requests/day
   125→
   126→---
   127→
   128→#### Mistral
   129→
   130→**Why Choose:** European AI company (GDPR-friendly). Privacy-focused.
   131→
   132→**Step-by-Step:**
   133→1. Go to [console.mistral.ai](https://console.mistral.ai)
   134→2. Click **Sign Up** → Sign up with Google/Email
   135→3. Verify your email
   136→4. Once logged in, go to [console.mistral.ai/api-keys/](https://console.mistral.ai/api-keys/)
   137→5. Click **Create new key** button
   138→6. Give it a name (e.g., "Neuman Brain")
   139→7. Copy the API key
   140→8. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings
   141→
   142→**Models Available:**
   143→- Mistral 7B (fast, free tier limited)
   144→- Mixtral 8x7B (MoE, paid)
   145→- Mistral Large (advanced, paid)
   146→
   147→**Free Tier:** Limited free requests, then pay-as-you-go
   148→
   149→**Pricing:** $0.25/1M tokens (Mistral 7B), $2/1M tokens (Mistral Large)
   150→
   151→---
   152→
   153→### 💳 Paid Providers (Bring Your Own Key)
   154→
   155→#### Google Gemini
   156→
   157→**Why Choose:** Massive 1M token context window. Multimodal (text + images).
   158→
   159→**Step-by-Step:**
   160→1. Go to [aistudio.google.com](https://aistudio.google.com)
   161→2. Sign in with Google account
   162→3. Click **Get API key** in left sidebar
   163→4. Click **Create API key** button
   164→5. Select a Google Cloud project (or create new one)
   165→6. Copy the API key (starts with `AIza...`)
   166→7. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings

   271→## Understanding Models
   272→
   273→### What Do the Ratings Mean?
   274→
   275→**Speed Rating (1-5):**
   276→- 1 = Slow (30+ seconds)
   277→- 3 = Moderate (5-10 seconds)
   278→- 5 = Lightning fast (<1 second)
   279→
   280→**Quality Rating (1-5):**
   281→- 1 = Basic responses
   282→- 3 = Good for most tasks
   283→- 5 = Excellent reasoning and accuracy
   284→
   285→### Choosing the Right Model
   286→
   287→**For Quick Questions:**
   288→- Use **fast** models (speed rating 4-5)
   289→- Examples: Groq Llama 3.3, Gemini Flash
   290→
   291→**For Complex Tasks:**
   292→- Use **quality** models (quality rating 4-5)
   293→- Examples: Claude 3.5 Sonnet, GPT-4o, Llama 3.3 70B
   294→
   295→**For Coding:**
   296→- Good options: GPT-4o, Claude, Llama 3.3 70B
   297→- Use cases: code generation, debugging, explanations
   298→
   299→**For Analysis:**
   300→- Best options: Claude 3.5 Sonnet, o1
   301→- Use cases: research, writing, deep reasoning
   302→
   303→**For Free Usage:**
   304→- OpenRouter Llama 3.3 70B (quality + free)
   305→- Groq models (speed + free)
   306→

   404→
   405→## Encryption & Security
   406→
   407→### Password Protection
   408→
   409→Your API keys are encrypted with **AES-256-CBC** encryption!
   410→
   411→**First Time Setup:**
   412→1. When you save your first API key, you'll be prompted to create a password
   413→2. Choose a **strong password** (min 12 characters)
   414→3. Select password expiry (daily, weekly, or monthly)
   415→4. Your password is stored **in memory only** (never saved to disk)
   416→
   417→**Password Requirements:**
   418→- Minimum 12 characters
   419→- Mix of uppercase, lowercase, numbers, symbols
   420→- Use a password manager!
   421→
   422→**Password Expiry:**
   423→- **Daily:** Re-enter password every 24 hours (most secure)
   424→- **Weekly:** Re-enter every 7 days (recommended)
   425→- **Monthly:** Re-enter every 30 days
   426→
   427→### How Encryption Works
   428→
   429→```
   430→Your API Key
   431→    ↓
   432→Encrypted with your password (AES-256-CBC)
   433→    ↓
   434→Stored in browser (encrypted)
   435→    ↓
   436→Decrypted when needed (in memory only)
   437→```
   438→
   439→**Your API keys are NEVER:**
   440→- ❌ Sent to our servers
   441→- ❌ Stored in plaintext
   442→- ❌ Shared with anyone
   443→
   444→**Your password is NEVER:**
   445→- ❌ Stored on disk
   446→- ❌ Sent over network
   447→- ❌ Recoverable if forgotten
   448→
   449→### Backup with .brain Files
   450→
   451→**Should you include encrypted keys in backups?**
   452→
   453→**✅ Safe if:**
   454→- You have a strong password
   455→- File stored securely
   456→- Only you know the password
   457→
   458→**⚠️ Risky if:**
   459→- Weak password
   460→- File shared/leaked
   461→- Others have access
   462→
   463→**Default:** Disabled (keys NOT exported)
   464→
   465→**To enable:**
   466→1. Go to AI Terminal Settings
   467→2. Look for "Export Encrypted Keys"
   468→3. Enable the toggle
   469→4. Your encrypted keys will be included in .brain exports
   470→

   474→
   475→### Getting Better Results
   476→

   478→- ❌ "Help with code"
   479→- ✅ "Write a React component that fetches data from an API and displays it in a table"
   480→
   481→**Provide context:**
   482→- Include error messages
   483→- Mention what you've tried
   484→- Share relevant code snippets
   485→
   486→**Use the right model:**
   487→- Quick questions → Fast models
   488→- Complex reasoning → Quality models
   489→- Code generation → Code-focused models
   490→
   491→### Managing Costs
   492→
   493→**Use free providers first:**
   494→- OpenRouter, Groq, HuggingFace are excellent and free!
   495→
   496→**Monitor usage:**
   497→- Check the Usage Tracker regularly
   498→- Set up multiple providers for redundancy
   499→- Use cheaper models for simple tasks

   501→**Estimated costs** (paid providers):
   502→- GPT-4o Mini: $0.15 per 1M tokens (~6,000 messages)
   503→- Claude 3.5 Haiku: $1 per 1M tokens (~1,000 messages)
   504→- GPT-4o: $2.50 per 1M tokens (~400 messages)

   506→### Security Best Practices
   507→
   508→1. **Use strong passwords** - 16+ characters with mix of types

   515→
   516→## Troubleshooting
   517→
   518→### "Provider not configured"
   519→
   520→**Problem:** You haven't added an API key for this provider.
   521→
   522→**Solution:**
   523→1. Click ⚙️ **Settings** in AI Terminal header
   524→2. Find the provider you want to use
   525→3. Click **Configure** or **Add API Key**
   526→4. Follow the provider-specific instructions above
   527→5. Click **Save** and then **Test Key** to verify
   528→6. Click **Done** to close settings
   529→
   530→---
   531→
   532→### "Invalid API key"
   533→
   534→**Problem:** The API key you entered is incorrect, expired, or revoked.
   535→
   536→**Common Causes:**
   537→- Typo when copying/pasting (extra space, missing character)
   538→- Wrong provider selected (e.g., using Groq key for OpenRouter)
   539→- API key revoked or deleted from provider dashboard
   540→- API key expired (some providers have expiry dates)
   541→- Payment method declined (for paid providers)
   542→
   543→**Solution:**
   544→1. **Double-check the key format:**
   545→   - OpenRouter: `sk-or-v1-...`
   546→   - Groq: `gsk_...`
   547→   - HuggingFace: `hf_...`
   548→   - OpenAI: `sk-...`
   549→   - Anthropic: `sk-ant-...`
   550→   - Gemini: `AIza...`
   551→2. **Copy/paste carefully** - Don't type it manually
   552→3. **Check provider dashboard:**
   553→   - Log in to the provider's website
   554→   - Navigate to API Keys section
   555→   - Verify the key still exists and is active
   556→4. **Regenerate key:**
   557→   - Delete old key in provider dashboard
   558→   - Create new API key
   559→   - Update it in Neuman Brain settings
   560→5. **For paid providers:** Verify payment method is valid
   561→
   562→---
   563→
   564→### "Failed to decrypt API key"
   565→
   566→**Problem:** Wrong password or corrupted encryption data.
   567→
   568→**Common Causes:**
   569→- Incorrect password (typo, caps lock, wrong keyboard layout)
   570→- Password changed since encryption
   571→- Browser storage corrupted
   572→- Different password used for different keys
   573→
   574→**Solution:**
   575→
   576→**Option 1: Try password again**
   577→1. Check **Caps Lock** is off
   578→2. Check keyboard layout (US vs international)
   579→3. Try typing password in a text editor first to verify
   580→4. Copy/paste password to ensure accuracy
   581→
   582→**Option 2: Reset encryption password**
   583→1. Click ⚙️ **Settings** in AI Terminal
   584→2. Scroll to **Security** section
   585→3. Click **Clear All Encrypted Keys** (⚠️ This removes ALL API keys!)
   586→4. Confirm deletion
   587→5. Re-add your API keys with new password
   588→6. Choose a password you'll remember (use password manager!)
   589→
   590→**Option 3: Check browser console for details**
   591→1. Open browser DevTools (F12)
   592→2. Go to **Console** tab
   593→3. Look for specific decryption error
   594→4. May indicate corrupted storage → Clear browser data
   595→
   596→**Prevention:**
   597→- Use a **password manager** (1Password, Bitwarden, LastPass)
   598→- Write password down securely (not on computer!)
   599→- Use consistent password across all keys
   600→
   601→---
   602→
   603→### "Rate limit exceeded"
   604→
   605→**Problem:** You've made too many requests to this provider in a short time.
   606→
   607→**Rate Limits by Provider:**
   608→- **OpenRouter Free:** 200 requests/minute
   609→- **Groq Free:** 30 requests/minute, 14,400 requests/day
   610→- **HuggingFace:** Varies by model (usually 1,000/day)
   611→- **Mistral Free:** ~100 requests/day
   612→- **Gemini Free:** 15 requests/minute (Flash), 2 requests/minute (Pro)
   613→- **OpenAI Tier 1:** 3,500 requests/day
   614→- **Anthropic Tier 1:** 50 requests/minute
   615→
   616→**Solution:**
   617→
   618→**Immediate:**
   619→1. **Wait** - Rate limits reset after time period (1 minute to 24 hours)
   620→2. **Automatic fallback** will switch to another provider
   621→3. **Check Usage Tracker** (📊) to see which provider has quota left
   622→
   623→**Long-term:**
   624→1. **Configure multiple providers:**
   625→   - Set up OpenRouter + Groq + HuggingFace (all free!)
   626→   - System automatically falls back when one hits limit
   627→2. **Upgrade to paid tier:**
   628→   - Paid providers have much higher limits (10,000+ requests/day)
   629→   - OpenRouter paid removes rate limits entirely
   630→3. **Batch your requests:**
   631→   - Instead of 10 quick questions, combine into one longer question
   632→   - Reduces API calls while getting same information
   633→4. **Use faster models:**
   634→   - Groq models respond in <1s, so you can ask 30 questions/minute vs 2/minute
   635→
   636→**Check remaining quota:**
   637→1. Visit provider dashboard (links above in "Getting API Keys")
   638→2. Look for "Usage" or "Billing" section
   639→3. See requests made today/this month
   640→
   641→---
   642→
   643→### "CORS error" (OpenAI, xAI)
   644→
   645→**Problem:** These providers don't allow direct browser API calls for security reasons.
   646→
   647→**Technical Details:**
   648→- CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) prevents browsers from calling certain APIs
   649→- OpenAI and xAI block browser requests to prevent API key theft
   650→- Anthropic, Google, OpenRouter DO support browser calls
   651→
   652→**Solution:**
   653→
   654→**Option 1: Use OpenRouter (RECOMMENDED)**
   655→- OpenRouter proxies to OpenAI models (GPT-4o, GPT-4o Mini, o1)
   656→- Works in browser with no CORS issues
   657→- Same models, same quality, easier to use
   658→- **Free tier available!**
   659→
   660→**Option 2: Use Anthropic Claude**
   661→- Claude 3.5 Sonnet is comparable to GPT-4o
   662→- Works directly in browser (CORS enabled)
   663→- Often considered higher quality for writing/analysis
   664→
   665→**Option 3: Wait for backend proxy**
   666→- We're planning a backend proxy service (Q2 2026)
   667→- Will enable direct OpenAI/xAI access from browser
   668→- Optional feature (privacy-preserving)
   669→
   670→**Not recommended:**
   671→- ❌ Browser extensions (security risk)
   672→- ❌ CORS unblocker extensions (breaks other sites)
   673→- ❌ Running local proxy (complex setup)
   674→
   675→---
   676→
   677→### Password Prompt Every Time
   678→
   679→**Problem:** You're being asked to enter your encryption password every session.
   680→
   681→**Why This Happens:**
   682→- Password expired (based on expiry setting: daily, weekly, monthly)
   683→- Browser session cleared (closed browser, cleared cookies)
   684→- Browser privacy mode (password never persists)
   685→- Different browser/device
   686→
   687→**Solution:**
   688→
   689→**Option 1: Change expiry duration**
   690→1. Next time you enter password, look for "Remember for" dropdown
   691→2. Change from **Daily** to **Weekly** or **Monthly**
   692→3. Trade-off: Longer duration = less secure but more convenient
   693→
   694→**Option 2: Use browser password manager**
   695→1. When prompted for password, let browser save it
   696→2. Browser will auto-fill on next prompt
   697→3. Still secure (browser encrypts saved passwords)
   698→
   699→**Option 3: Accept it (most secure)**
   700→- Daily re-entry is the MOST secure option
   701→- Prevents unauthorized access if device stolen
   702→- Recommended for sensitive API keys
   703→
   704→**Not recommended:**
   705→- ❌ Disabling encryption (API keys stored in plaintext!)
   706→- ❌ Using weak password for convenience (defeats encryption)
   707→
   708→---
   709→

   711→
   712→**Problem:** AI responses taking 10+ seconds to appear.
   713→
   714→**Common Causes:**
   715→- Using slow model (quality rating high, speed rating low)
   716→- Large context (long conversation history)
   717→- Provider experiencing high load

   719→
   720→**Solution:**
   721→
   722→**Option 1: Switch to faster model**

   725→3. Select Groq Llama 3.3 or Gemini Flash
   726→4. Should see responses in <1 second
   727→
   728→**Option 2: Clear conversation history**
   729→1. Click **🗑️** trash icon to clear chat
   730→2. Shorter context = faster responses
   731→3. Long conversations increase processing time
   732→
   733→**Option 3: Try different provider**
   734→1. Check if other providers are faster right now
   735→2. OpenRouter → Groq → HuggingFace
   736→3. Some providers have peak/off-peak hours
   737→
   738→**Speed Comparison:**
   739→- **Groq:** <1 second (custom LPU hardware)
   740→- **Gemini Flash:** 1-3 seconds
   741→- **Claude 3.5 Haiku:** 2-4 seconds
   742→- **GPT-4o Mini:** 3-5 seconds
   743→- **Claude 3.5 Sonnet:** 5-8 seconds
   744→- **GPT-4o:** 8-12 seconds
   745→- **o1:** 30-60 seconds (reasoning model)
   746→
   747→---
   748→
   749→### "Network error" or "Failed to fetch"
   750→
   751→**Problem:** Cannot connect to AI provider.
   752→
   753→**Common Causes:**
   754→- Internet connection down
   755→- Provider API server down
   756→- Firewall blocking API calls
   757→- VPN interfering with connection
   758→
   759→**Solution:**
   760→
   761→**Option 1: Check internet connection**
   762→1. Open another website (google.com)
   763→2. If it loads, internet is working
   764→3. If not, fix internet connection first
   765→
   766→**Option 2: Check provider status**
   767→1. Visit provider status page:
   768→   - OpenRouter: [status.openrouter.ai](https://status.openrouter.ai)
   769→   - Groq: [status.groq.com](https://status.groq.com)
   770→   - OpenAI: [status.openai.com](https://status.openai.com)
   771→   - Anthropic: [status.anthropic.com](https://status.anthropic.com)
   772→2. If provider is down, automatic fallback will activate
   773→3. Or manually switch to another provider
   774→
   775→**Option 3: Disable VPN temporarily**
   776→1. Some VPNs block AI API endpoints
   777→2. Try disabling VPN
   778→3. If it works, whitelist the AI provider domains
   779→
   780→**Option 4: Check browser console**
   781→1. Open DevTools (F12) → Console tab
   782→2. Look for specific error message
   783→3. May indicate firewall or network policy blocking
   784→
   785→**Option 5: Try different browser**
   786→- Some corporate firewalls block in specific browsers
   787→- Try Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari
   788→

   790→
   791→### Responses in Wrong Language
   792→
   793→**Problem:** AI responding in a language you didn't request.
   794→
   795→**Common Causes:**
   796→- Model defaulting to its training language
   797→- Previous conversation context
   798→- Browser language settings
   799→
   800→**Solution:**
   801→
   802→**Option 1: Explicitly specify language**
   803→```
   804→Please respond in English.
   805→[Your question here]
   806→```
   807→
   808→**Option 2: Use English-first models**
   809→- Llama 3.3 (English-first)
   810→- GPT-4o (English-first)
   811→- Claude (English-first)
   812→- Avoid: Qwen (Chinese-first), Mistral (French-first)
   813→
   814→**Option 3: Clear conversation and restart**
   815→1. Click 🗑️ to clear chat history
   816→2. Start fresh conversation in desired language
   817→3. Model won't have conflicting context
   818→
   819→---
   820→
   821→### API Key Compromise / Unauthorized Usage
   822→
   823→**Problem:** You suspect your API key has been stolen or leaked.
   824→
   825→**Immediate Actions:**
   826→1. **Revoke key immediately** in provider dashboard
   827→2. **Generate new key**
   828→3. **Check usage/billing** in provider dashboard for suspicious activity
   829→4. **Report to provider** if unauthorized charges occurred
   830→5. **Update key** in Neuman Brain settings
   831→
   832→**Prevention:**
   833→- ✅ Never commit API keys to GitHub/GitLab
   834→- ✅ Never share .brain files with encrypted keys enabled
   835→- ✅ Use strong encryption password (16+ characters)
   836→- ✅ Rotate keys every 3-6 months
   837→- ✅ Monitor provider dashboards for unusual activity
   838→- ✅ Set spending limits in provider dashboards (if available)
   839→
   840→**Spending Limits:**
   841→- OpenAI: Settings → Billing → Usage limits
   842→- Anthropic: Settings → Billing → Set budget
   843→- Google: Cloud Console → Billing → Budgets
   844→
   845→---
   846→
   847→## Keyboard Shortcuts
   848→
   849→- **Enter** - Send message
   850→- **Shift+Enter** - New line in message
   851→- **Esc** - Close modal/panel (when focused)
   852→
   853→---
   854→
   855→## FAQs
   856→
   857→### Is my data private?
   858→
   859→**Yes!** Everything is stored locally in your browser:
   860→- Chat history → Browser storage
   861→- API keys → Encrypted in browser storage
   862→- No cloud storage, no server tracking
   863→
   864→### Can I use multiple providers at once?
   865→
   866→**Yes!** Configure as many providers as you want. The system uses one at a time but automatically falls back if needed.
   867→
   868→### What happens if I forget my password?
   869→
   870→You'll need to clear your API keys and re-add them with a new password. Your chat history is NOT affected.
   871→
   872→### How do I remove a provider?
   873→
   874→1. Open AI Terminal
   875→2. Click ⚙️ Settings
   876→3. Find the provider
   877→4. Click **Remove** button
   878→
   879→### Can I export my chat history?
   880→
   881→Yes! Use the .brain export feature in Settings. Your entire chat history is included.
   882→
   883→### Which provider is best?
   884→
   885→**For free:** OpenRouter (most versatile) or Groq (fastest)
   886→**For paid:** Claude 3.5 Sonnet (best quality) or GPT-4o (most popular)
   887→
   888→### Do I need to pay for AI providers?
   889→
   890→No! 4 providers offer free tiers:
   891→- OpenRouter
   892→- Groq
   893→- HuggingFace
   894→- Mistral
   895→
   896→### How much do paid providers cost?
   897→
   898→Typically $0.15-$15 per 1 million tokens. For reference:
   899→- 1M tokens ≈ 750,000 words
   900→- Average conversation: 500-2000 tokens
   901→- You can have hundreds/thousands of conversations for $1
   902→
   903→---
   904→
   905→## Getting Help
   906→
   907→### Provider Issues
   908→
   909→Each provider has their own support:
   910→- **OpenRouter:** [openrouter.ai/docs](https://openrouter.ai/docs)
   911→- **Groq:** [console.groq.com/docs](https://console.groq.com/docs)
   912→- **HuggingFace:** [huggingface.co/docs](https://huggingface.co/docs)
   913→- **Mistral:** [docs.mistral.ai](https://docs.mistral.ai)
   914→- **OpenAI:** [platform.openai.com/docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs)
   915→- **Anthropic:** [docs.anthropic.com](https://docs.anthropic.com)
   916→- **Gemini:** [ai.google.dev/docs](https://ai.google.dev/docs)
   917→- **xAI:** [docs.x.ai](https://docs.x.ai)
   918→
   919→### Technical Issues
   920→
   921→For bugs or feature requests, please file an issue on our GitHub repository.
   922→
   923→---
   924→
   925→## Changelog
   926→
   927→### Version 2.0 (November 2025)
   928→- ✅ Multi-provider support (8 providers)
   929→- ✅ Encrypted API key storage
   930→- ✅ Automatic fallback system
   931→- ✅ Usage tracking per provider
   932→- ✅ Model selector with filtering
   933→- ✅ Free tier support
   934→
   935→### Version 1.0 (Previous)
   936→- Single provider (Google Gemini)
   937→- Plaintext API key storage
   938→- No fallback
   939→
   940→---
   941→
   942→**Enjoy your AI Terminal!** 🚀
   943→
   944→If you have questions or feedback, we'd love to hear from you.
   945→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

# AI Terminal User Guide

**Welcome to the Multi-Provider AI Terminal!**

Get help with coding, questions, productivity, and more using 8 different AI providers with automatic fallback and encrypted key storage.

---

## Quick Start

### Step 1: Open the AI Terminal

Click the **🤖 AI Terminal** button in the bottom-right corner of the screen.

### Step 2: Configure a Provider

1. Click the **⚙️ Settings** button in the terminal header
2. Choose a provider (see recommendations below)
3. Get an API key from the provider's website
4. Enter your API key and click **Save**
5. Click **Test Key** to verify it works
6. Click **Done** to close settings

### Step 3: Start Chatting!

Type your question or request and press Enter. The AI will respond in real-time!

---

## Choosing a Provider

### Cost Comparison Table

| Provider | Free Tier | Paid Pricing | Credit Card Required | Best For |
|----------|-----------|--------------|---------------------|----------|
| **OpenRouter** | ✅ Many free models | $0.06-$30/1M tokens | ❌ No | Most versatile |
| **Groq** | ✅ Generous free tier | N/A (free only) | ❌ No | Fastest speed |
| **HuggingFace** | ✅ True free tier | N/A (free only) | ❌ No | Open source |
| **Mistral** | ⚠️ Limited free | $0.25-$2/1M tokens | ⚠️ Yes | GDPR compliance |
| **Gemini** | ✅ Free tier | $0.075-$1.25/1M tokens | ⚠️ Yes | Large context |
| **OpenAI** | ❌ No | $0.15-$15/1M tokens | ✅ Yes | GPT-4o, o1 |
| **Anthropic** | ❌ No | $1-$15/1M tokens | ✅ Yes | Best quality |
| **xAI** | ❌ No | ~$5/1M tokens | ✅ Yes | Real-time info |

**💡 Tip:** Start with OpenRouter or Groq (both free, no credit card needed)

---

## Getting API Keys: Step-by-Step

### 🆓 Free Providers (Recommended to Start)

#### OpenRouter

**Why Choose:** Access to 200+ models (including GPT-4o, Claude, Llama) through one API. Many free models available.

**Step-by-Step:**
1. Go to [openrouter.ai](https://openrouter.ai)
2. Click **Sign In** (top right) → Sign in with Google/GitHub/Email
3. Once logged in, go to [openrouter.ai/keys](https://openrouter.ai/keys)
4. Click **Create Key** button
5. Give it a name (e.g., "Neuman Brain Dashboard")
6. Copy the API key (starts with `sk-or-v1-...`)
7. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings

**Free Models Available:**
- Llama 3.3 70B Instruct (quality + free)
- Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking (Google's latest, free)
- Mistral 7B (fast, free)
- Qwen 2.5 72B (multilingual, free)

**Rate Limits:** 200 requests/minute (generous!)

---

#### Groq

**Why Choose:** Fastest AI inference in the world (custom LPU hardware). 100% free.

**Step-by-Step:**
1. Go to [console.groq.com](https://console.groq.com)
2. Click **Sign Up** → Sign up with Google/Email
3. Verify your email (check inbox)
4. Once logged in, click **API Keys** in left sidebar
5. Click **Create API Key** button
6. Give it a name (e.g., "Neuman Brain")
7. Copy the API key (starts with `gsk_...`)
8. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings

**Free Models Available:**
- Llama 3.3 70B (Meta's latest, free)
- Llama 3.1 8B (fast, free)
- Mixtral 8x7B (Mistral's MoE, free)
- Gemma 2 9B (Google, free)

**Rate Limits:** 30 requests/minute, 14,400 requests/day (very generous!)

**Speed:** Responses in <1 second (seriously!)

---

#### HuggingFace

**Why Choose:** Thousands of open-source models. True free tier with no credit card required.

**Step-by-Step:**
1. Go to [huggingface.co/join](https://huggingface.co/join)
2. Sign up with Email
3. Verify your email (check inbox)
4. Once logged in, go to [huggingface.co/settings/tokens](https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens)
5. Click **New token** button
6. Give it a name (e.g., "Neuman Brain Dashboard")
7. Select **Read** permission (sufficient for inference)
8. Click **Generate token**
9. Copy the token (starts with `hf_...`)
10. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings

**Popular Models:**
- Meta Llama models (various sizes)
- Mistral models
- Falcon models
- Many community fine-tunes

**Rate Limits:** Varies by model, generally 1,000+ requests/day

---

#### Mistral

**Why Choose:** European AI company (GDPR-friendly). Privacy-focused.

**Step-by-Step:**
1. Go to [console.mistral.ai](https://console.mistral.ai)
2. Click **Sign Up** → Sign up with Google/Email
3. Verify your email
4. Once logged in, go to [console.mistral.ai/api-keys/](https://console.mistral.ai/api-keys/)
5. Click **Create new key** button
6. Give it a name (e.g., "Neuman Brain")
7. Copy the API key
8. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings

**Models Available:**
- Mistral 7B (fast, free tier limited)
- Mixtral 8x7B (MoE, paid)
- Mistral Large (advanced, paid)

**Free Tier:** Limited free requests, then pay-as-you-go

**Pricing:** $0.25/1M tokens (Mistral 7B), $2/1M tokens (Mistral Large)

---

### 💳 Paid Providers (Bring Your Own Key)

#### Google Gemini

**Why Choose:** Massive 1M token context window. Multimodal (text + images).

**Step-by-Step:**
1. Go to [aistudio.google.com](https://aistudio.google.com)
2. Sign in with Google account
3. Click **Get API key** in left sidebar
4. Click **Create API key** button
5. Select a Google Cloud project (or create new one)
6. Copy the API key (starts with `AIza...`)
7. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings

**Models Available:**
- Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking (latest, fast, $0.075/1M tokens)
- Gemini 1.5 Pro (advanced, $1.25/1M tokens)
- Gemini 1.5 Flash (fast, $0.075/1M tokens)

**Free Tier:** 15 requests/minute, 1,500 requests/day (Flash), 2 requests/minute (Pro)

**Pricing:**
- Flash: $0.075/1M input tokens, $0.30/1M output tokens
- Pro: $1.25/1M input tokens, $5/1M output tokens

**Context Window:** Up to 1 million tokens (equivalent to 10 novels!)

---

#### OpenAI

**Why Choose:** GPT-4o (best coding model), o1 (best reasoning model).

**Step-by-Step:**
1. Go to [platform.openai.com/signup](https://platform.openai.com/signup)
2. Sign up with Email/Google/Microsoft
3. Verify your email
4. Add payment method (credit card required for API access)
5. Once logged in, go to [platform.openai.com/api-keys](https://platform.openai.com/api-keys)
6. Click **Create new secret key** button
7. Give it a name (e.g., "Neuman Brain Dashboard")
8. Copy the API key (starts with `sk-...`)
9. **Important:** Save it NOW (you can't view it again!)
10. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings

**Models Available:**
- GPT-4o (best overall, $2.50/1M tokens)
- GPT-4o Mini (fast, cheap, $0.15/1M tokens)
- o1 (reasoning, $15/1M tokens)
- o1-mini (reasoning, cheaper, $3/1M tokens)

**Note:** OpenAI requires backend proxy due to CORS. Use OpenRouter to access OpenAI models instead!

**Pricing:**
- GPT-4o Mini: $0.15/1M input, $0.60/1M output
- GPT-4o: $2.50/1M input, $10/1M output
- o1: $15/1M input, $60/1M output

**Credit Required:** $5 minimum deposit

---

#### Anthropic Claude

**Why Choose:** Best quality responses for writing, analysis, coding. Works directly in browser!

**Step-by-Step:**
1. Go to [console.anthropic.com](https://console.anthropic.com)
2. Click **Sign Up** → Sign up with Email
3. Verify your email
4. Add payment method (credit card required)
5. Once logged in, go to [console.anthropic.com/settings/keys](https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys)
6. Click **Create Key** button
7. Give it a name (e.g., "Neuman Brain")
8. Copy the API key (starts with `sk-ant-...`)
9. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings

**Models Available:**
- Claude 3.5 Sonnet (best quality, $3/1M tokens)
- Claude 3.5 Haiku (fast, cheap, $1/1M tokens)
- Claude 3 Opus (legacy, expensive, $15/1M tokens)

**Pricing:**
- Haiku: $1/1M input, $5/1M output
- Sonnet: $3/1M input, $15/1M output
- Opus: $15/1M input, $75/1M output

**Credit Required:** $5 minimum deposit

**Special:** Claude works directly in browser (CORS enabled) unlike OpenAI!

---

#### xAI Grok

**Why Choose:** Unique personality, real-time information access (X/Twitter integration).

**Step-by-Step:**
1. Go to [console.x.ai](https://console.x.ai)
2. Sign in with X (Twitter) account
3. Navigate to API Keys section
4. Click **Create API Key**
5. Copy the API key
6. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings

**Models Available:**
- Grok Beta (general purpose)
- Grok Vision (multimodal - coming soon)

**Note:** xAI requires backend proxy due to CORS limitations.

**Pricing:** Estimated $5/1M tokens (subject to change)

**Credit Required:** Payment details needed

---

## Understanding Models

### What Do the Ratings Mean?

**Speed Rating (1-5):**
- 1 = Slow (30+ seconds)
- 3 = Moderate (5-10 seconds)
- 5 = Lightning fast (<1 second)

**Quality Rating (1-5):**
- 1 = Basic responses
- 3 = Good for most tasks
- 5 = Excellent reasoning and accuracy

### Choosing the Right Model

**For Quick Questions:**
- Use **fast** models (speed rating 4-5)
- Examples: Groq Llama 3.3, Gemini Flash

**For Complex Tasks:**
- Use **quality** models (quality rating 4-5)
- Examples: Claude 3.5 Sonnet, GPT-4o, Llama 3.3 70B

**For Coding:**
- Good options: GPT-4o, Claude, Llama 3.3 70B
- Use cases: code generation, debugging, explanations

**For Analysis:**
- Best options: Claude 3.5 Sonnet, o1
- Use cases: research, writing, deep reasoning

**For Free Usage:**
- OpenRouter Llama 3.3 70B (quality + free)
- Groq models (speed + free)

---

### Best Model for Each Task Type

| Task Type | Best Free Model | Best Paid Model | Why |
|-----------|----------------|-----------------|-----|
| **Code Generation** | Llama 3.3 70B (OpenRouter) | GPT-4o | Strong programming knowledge |
| **Code Debugging** | Llama 3.3 70B (Groq) | Claude 3.5 Sonnet | Excellent error analysis |
| **Writing & Editing** | Llama 3.3 70B (OpenRouter) | Claude 3.5 Sonnet | Best prose quality |
| **Research & Analysis** | Qwen 2.5 72B (OpenRouter) | o1 | Deep reasoning |
| **Quick Q&A** | Gemini Flash (OpenRouter) | Claude 3.5 Haiku | Fast + accurate |
| **Brainstorming** | Mixtral 8x7B (Groq) | GPT-4o | Creative responses |
| **Math & Logic** | Llama 3.3 70B (Groq) | o1 | Step-by-step reasoning |
| **Multilingual** | Qwen 2.5 72B (OpenRouter) | Gemini Pro | 100+ languages |
| **Long Context** | Gemini Flash (free tier) | Gemini Pro | 1M token window |
| **Conversation** | Llama 3.3 70B (Groq) | Claude 3.5 Sonnet | Natural dialogue |

**Cost-Optimized Strategy:**
1. **Free Daily Use:** OpenRouter Llama 3.3 70B for everything
2. **Need Speed:** Groq Llama 3.3 70B (same model, 10x faster)
3. **Complex Reasoning:** Upgrade to Claude 3.5 Sonnet or o1 (only when needed)
4. **Huge Context:** Use Gemini Flash free tier (1M tokens)

---

## Using the AI Terminal

### Basic Chat

Just type your question and press Enter!

**Examples:**
```
You: What's the difference between React and Vue?

You: Write a function to validate email addresses in JavaScript

You: Help me debug this error: "Cannot read property 'map' of undefined"
```

### Streaming Responses

All providers support streaming (except o1 models), so you'll see responses appear in real-time!

### Multi-Line Input

- Press **Shift+Enter** for new line
- Press **Enter** alone to send message

### Clear Chat

Click the **🗑️** button to clear chat history.

---

## Advanced Features

### Model Selection

Click **🔀 Model Selector** to:
- Browse all available models
- Filter by provider, use case, or free/paid
- Compare speed, quality, context, and cost
- Switch models anytime

### Usage Tracking

Click **📊 Usage Tracker** to see:
- Requests per provider
- Token usage (estimated)
- Cost estimates (for paid providers)
- Time range filters (today, week, month, all)

### Automatic Fallback

If your primary provider fails (rate limit, quota, network error), the system automatically tries backup providers!

**You'll see a notification like:**
> ⚠️ openrouter failed: Rate limit exceeded. Switched to groq (llama-3.3-70b).

**Fallback order:**
1. OpenRouter
2. Groq
3. HuggingFace
4. Mistral
5. Gemini

### Provider Information

Each message shows which provider and model was used:

```
🤖 [Assistant Response]
12:34 PM • openrouter • llama-3.3-70b
```

---

## Encryption & Security

### Password Protection

Your API keys are encrypted with **AES-256-CBC** encryption!

**First Time Setup:**
1. When you save your first API key, you'll be prompted to create a password
2. Choose a **strong password** (min 12 characters)
3. Select password expiry (daily, weekly, or monthly)
4. Your password is stored **in memory only** (never saved to disk)

**Password Requirements:**
- Minimum 12 characters
- Mix of uppercase, lowercase, numbers, symbols
- Use a password manager!

**Password Expiry:**
- **Daily:** Re-enter password every 24 hours (most secure)
- **Weekly:** Re-enter every 7 days (recommended)
- **Monthly:** Re-enter every 30 days

### How Encryption Works

```
Your API Key
    ↓
Encrypted with your password (AES-256-CBC)
    ↓
Stored in browser (encrypted)
    ↓
Decrypted when needed (in memory only)
```

**Your API keys are NEVER:**
- ❌ Sent to our servers
- ❌ Stored in plaintext
- ❌ Shared with anyone

**Your password is NEVER:**
- ❌ Stored on disk
- ❌ Sent over network
- ❌ Recoverable if forgotten

### Backup with .brain Files

**Should you include encrypted keys in backups?**

**✅ Safe if:**
- You have a strong password
- File stored securely
- Only you know the password

**⚠️ Risky if:**
- Weak password
- File shared/leaked
- Others have access

**Default:** Disabled (keys NOT exported)

**To enable:**
1. Go to AI Terminal Settings
2. Look for "Export Encrypted Keys"
3. Enable the toggle
4. Your encrypted keys will be included in .brain exports

---

## Tips & Best Practices

### Getting Better Results

**Be specific:**
- ❌ "Help with code"
- ✅ "Write a React component that fetches data from an API and displays it in a table"

**Provide context:**
- Include error messages
- Mention what you've tried
- Share relevant code snippets

**Use the right model:**
- Quick questions → Fast models
- Complex reasoning → Quality models
- Code generation → Code-focused models

### Managing Costs

**Use free providers first:**
- OpenRouter, Groq, HuggingFace are excellent and free!

**Monitor usage:**
- Check the Usage Tracker regularly
- Set up multiple providers for redundancy
- Use cheaper models for simple tasks

**Estimated costs** (paid providers):
- GPT-4o Mini: $0.15 per 1M tokens (~6,000 messages)
- Claude 3.5 Haiku: $1 per 1M tokens (~1,000 messages)
- GPT-4o: $2.50 per 1M tokens (~400 messages)

### Security Best Practices

1. **Use strong passwords** - 16+ characters with mix of types
2. **Don't share .brain files** - Especially with encrypted keys enabled
3. **Rotate API keys** - Change them every few months
4. **Monitor API usage** - Check provider dashboards for suspicious activity
5. **Use free tiers first** - Test before committing to paid services

---

## Troubleshooting

### "Provider not configured"

**Problem:** You haven't added an API key for this provider.

**Solution:**
1. Click ⚙️ **Settings** in AI Terminal header
2. Find the provider you want to use
3. Click **Configure** or **Add API Key**
4. Follow the provider-specific instructions above
5. Click **Save** and then **Test Key** to verify
6. Click **Done** to close settings

---

### "Invalid API key"

**Problem:** The API key you entered is incorrect, expired, or revoked.

**Common Causes:**
- Typo when copying/pasting (extra space, missing character)
- Wrong provider selected (e.g., using Groq key for OpenRouter)
- API key revoked or deleted from provider dashboard
- API key expired (some providers have expiry dates)
- Payment method declined (for paid providers)

**Solution:**
1. **Double-check the key format:**
   - OpenRouter: `sk-or-v1-...`
   - Groq: `gsk_...`
   - HuggingFace: `hf_...`
   - OpenAI: `sk-...`
   - Anthropic: `sk-ant-...`
   - Gemini: `AIza...`
2. **Copy/paste carefully** - Don't type it manually
3. **Check provider dashboard:**
   - Log in to the provider's website
   - Navigate to API Keys section
   - Verify the key still exists and is active
4. **Regenerate key:**
   - Delete old key in provider dashboard
   - Create new API key
   - Update it in Neuman Brain settings
5. **For paid providers:** Verify payment method is valid

---

### "Failed to decrypt API key"

**Problem:** Wrong password or corrupted encryption data.

**Common Causes:**
- Incorrect password (typo, caps lock, wrong keyboard layout)
- Password changed since encryption
- Browser storage corrupted
- Different password used for different keys

**Solution:**

**Option 1: Try password again**
1. Check **Caps Lock** is off
2. Check keyboard layout (US vs international)
3. Try typing password in a text editor first to verify
4. Copy/paste password to ensure accuracy

**Option 2: Reset encryption password**
1. Click ⚙️ **Settings** in AI Terminal
2. Scroll to **Security** section
3. Click **Clear All Encrypted Keys** (⚠️ This removes ALL API keys!)
4. Confirm deletion
5. Re-add your API keys with new password
6. Choose a password you'll remember (use password manager!)

**Option 3: Check browser console for details**
1. Open browser DevTools (F12)
2. Go to **Console** tab
3. Look for specific decryption error
4. May indicate corrupted storage → Clear browser data

**Prevention:**
- Use a **password manager** (1Password, Bitwarden, LastPass)
- Write password down securely (not on computer!)
- Use consistent password across all keys

---

### "Rate limit exceeded"

**Problem:** You've made too many requests to this provider in a short time.

**Rate Limits by Provider:**
- **OpenRouter Free:** 200 requests/minute
- **Groq Free:** 30 requests/minute, 14,400 requests/day
- **HuggingFace:** Varies by model (usually 1,000/day)
- **Mistral Free:** ~100 requests/day
- **Gemini Free:** 15 requests/minute (Flash), 2 requests/minute (Pro)
- **OpenAI Tier 1:** 3,500 requests/day
- **Anthropic Tier 1:** 50 requests/minute

**Solution:**

**Immediate:**
1. **Wait** - Rate limits reset after time period (1 minute to 24 hours)
2. **Automatic fallback** will switch to another provider
3. **Check Usage Tracker** (📊) to see which provider has quota left

**Long-term:**
1. **Configure multiple providers:**
   - Set up OpenRouter + Groq + HuggingFace (all free!)
   - System automatically falls back when one hits limit
2. **Upgrade to paid tier:**
   - Paid providers have much higher limits (10,000+ requests/day)
   - OpenRouter paid removes rate limits entirely
3. **Batch your requests:**
   - Instead of 10 quick questions, combine into one longer question
   - Reduces API calls while getting same information
4. **Use faster models:**
   - Groq models respond in <1s, so you can ask 30 questions/minute vs 2/minute

**Check remaining quota:**
1. Visit provider dashboard (links above in "Getting API Keys")
2. Look for "Usage" or "Billing" section
3. See requests made today/this month

---

### "CORS error" (OpenAI, xAI)

**Problem:** These providers don't allow direct browser API calls for security reasons.

**Technical Details:**
- CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) prevents browsers from calling certain APIs
- OpenAI and xAI block browser requests to prevent API key theft
- Anthropic, Google, OpenRouter DO support browser calls

**Solution:**

**Option 1: Use OpenRouter (RECOMMENDED)**
- OpenRouter proxies to OpenAI models (GPT-4o, GPT-4o Mini, o1)
- Works in browser with no CORS issues
- Same models, same quality, easier to use
- **Free tier available!**

**Option 2: Use Anthropic Claude**
- Claude 3.5 Sonnet is comparable to GPT-4o
- Works directly in browser (CORS enabled)
- Often considered higher quality for writing/analysis

**Option 3: Wait for backend proxy**
- We're planning a backend proxy service (Q2 2026)
- Will enable direct OpenAI/xAI access from browser
- Optional feature (privacy-preserving)

**Not recommended:**
- ❌ Browser extensions (security risk)
- ❌ CORS unblocker extensions (breaks other sites)
- ❌ Running local proxy (complex setup)

---

### Password Prompt Every Time

**Problem:** You're being asked to enter your encryption password every session.

**Why This Happens:**
- Password expired (based on expiry setting: daily, weekly, monthly)
- Browser session cleared (closed browser, cleared cookies)
- Browser privacy mode (password never persists)
- Different browser/device

**Solution:**

**Option 1: Change expiry duration**
1. Next time you enter password, look for "Remember for" dropdown
2. Change from **Daily** to **Weekly** or **Monthly**
3. Trade-off: Longer duration = less secure but more convenient

**Option 2: Use browser password manager**
1. When prompted for password, let browser save it
2. Browser will auto-fill on next prompt
3. Still secure (browser encrypts saved passwords)

**Option 3: Accept it (most secure)**
- Daily re-entry is the MOST secure option
- Prevents unauthorized access if device stolen
- Recommended for sensitive API keys

**Not recommended:**
- ❌ Disabling encryption (API keys stored in plaintext!)
- ❌ Using weak password for convenience (defeats encryption)

---

### Slow Responses

**Problem:** AI responses taking 10+ seconds to appear.

**Common Causes:**
- Using slow model (quality rating high, speed rating low)
- Large context (long conversation history)
- Provider experiencing high load
- Network latency

**Solution:**

**Option 1: Switch to faster model**
1. Click **🔀 Model Selector**
2. Filter by **Speed** rating (4-5 stars)
3. Select Groq Llama 3.3 or Gemini Flash
4. Should see responses in <1 second

**Option 2: Clear conversation history**
1. Click **🗑️** trash icon to clear chat
2. Shorter context = faster responses
3. Long conversations increase processing time

**Option 3: Try different provider**
1. Check if other providers are faster right now
2. OpenRouter → Groq → HuggingFace
3. Some providers have peak/off-peak hours

**Speed Comparison:**
- **Groq:** <1 second (custom LPU hardware)
- **Gemini Flash:** 1-3 seconds
- **Claude 3.5 Haiku:** 2-4 seconds
- **GPT-4o Mini:** 3-5 seconds
- **Claude 3.5 Sonnet:** 5-8 seconds
- **GPT-4o:** 8-12 seconds
- **o1:** 30-60 seconds (reasoning model)

---

### "Network error" or "Failed to fetch"

**Problem:** Cannot connect to AI provider.

**Common Causes:**
- Internet connection down
- Provider API server down
- Firewall blocking API calls
- VPN interfering with connection

**Solution:**

**Option 1: Check internet connection**
1. Open another website (google.com)
2. If it loads, internet is working
3. If not, fix internet connection first

**Option 2: Check provider status**
1. Visit provider status page:
   - OpenRouter: [status.openrouter.ai](https://status.openrouter.ai)
   - Groq: [status.groq.com](https://status.groq.com)
   - OpenAI: [status.openai.com](https://status.openai.com)
   - Anthropic: [status.anthropic.com](https://status.anthropic.com)
2. If provider is down, automatic fallback will activate
3. Or manually switch to another provider

**Option 3: Disable VPN temporarily**
1. Some VPNs block AI API endpoints
2. Try disabling VPN
3. If it works, whitelist the AI provider domains

**Option 4: Check browser console**
1. Open DevTools (F12) → Console tab
2. Look for specific error message
3. May indicate firewall or network policy blocking

**Option 5: Try different browser**
- Some corporate firewalls block in specific browsers
- Try Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari

---

### Responses in Wrong Language

**Problem:** AI responding in a language you didn't request.

**Common Causes:**
- Model defaulting to its training language
- Previous conversation context
- Browser language settings

**Solution:**

**Option 1: Explicitly specify language**
```
Please respond in English.
[Your question here]
```

**Option 2: Use English-first models**
- Llama 3.3 (English-first)
- GPT-4o (English-first)
- Claude (English-first)
- Avoid: Qwen (Chinese-first), Mistral (French-first)

**Option 3: Clear conversation and restart**
1. Click 🗑️ to clear chat history
2. Start fresh conversation in desired language
3. Model won't have conflicting context

---

### API Key Compromise / Unauthorized Usage

**Problem:** You suspect your API key has been stolen or leaked.

**Immediate Actions:**
1. **Revoke key immediately** in provider dashboard
2. **Generate new key**
3. **Check usage/billing** in provider dashboard for suspicious activity
4. **Report to provider** if unauthorized charges occurred
5. **Update key** in Neuman Brain settings

**Prevention:**
- ✅ Never commit API keys to GitHub/GitLab
- ✅ Never share .brain files with encrypted keys enabled
- ✅ Use strong encryption password (16+ characters)
- ✅ Rotate keys every 3-6 months
- ✅ Monitor provider dashboards for unusual activity
- ✅ Set spending limits in provider dashboards (if available)

**Spending Limits:**
- OpenAI: Settings → Billing → Usage limits
- Anthropic: Settings → Billing → Set budget
- Google: Cloud Console → Billing → Budgets

---

## Keyboard Shortcuts

- **Enter** - Send message
- **Shift+Enter** - New line in message
- **Esc** - Close modal/panel (when focused)

---

## FAQs

### Is my data private?

**Yes!** Everything is stored locally in your browser:
- Chat history → Browser storage
- API keys → Encrypted in browser storage
- No cloud storage, no server tracking

### Can I use multiple providers at once?

**Yes!** Configure as many providers as you want. The system uses one at a time but automatically falls back if needed.

### What happens if I forget my password?

You'll need to clear your API keys and re-add them with a new password. Your chat history is NOT affected.

### How do I remove a provider?

1. Open AI Terminal
2. Click ⚙️ Settings
3. Find the provider
4. Click **Remove** button

### Can I export my chat history?

Yes! Use the .brain export feature in Settings. Your entire chat history is included.

### Which provider is best?

**For free:** OpenRouter (most versatile) or Groq (fastest)
**For paid:** Claude 3.5 Sonnet (best quality) or GPT-4o (most popular)

### Do I need to pay for AI providers?

No! 4 providers offer free tiers:
- OpenRouter
- Groq
- HuggingFace
- Mistral

### How much do paid providers cost?

Typically $0.15-$15 per 1 million tokens. For reference:
- 1M tokens ≈ 750,000 words
- Average conversation: 500-2000 tokens
- You can have hundreds/thousands of conversations for $1

---

## Getting Help

### Provider Issues

Each provider has their own support:
- **OpenRouter:** [openrouter.ai/docs](https://openrouter.ai/docs)
- **Groq:** [console.groq.com/docs](https://console.groq.com/docs)
- **HuggingFace:** [huggingface.co/docs](https://huggingface.co/docs)
- **Mistral:** [docs.mistral.ai](https://docs.mistral.ai)
- **OpenAI:** [platform.openai.com/docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs)
- **Anthropic:** [docs.anthropic.com](https://docs.anthropic.com)
- **Gemini:** [ai.google.dev/docs](https://ai.google.dev/docs)
- **xAI:** [docs.x.ai](https://docs.x.ai)

### Technical Issues

For bugs or feature requests, please file an issue on our GitHub repository.

---

## Changelog

### Version 2.0 (November 2025)
- ✅ Multi-provider support (8 providers)
- ✅ Encrypted API key storage
- ✅ Automatic fallback system
- ✅ Usage tracking per provider
- ✅ Model selector with filtering
- ✅ Free tier support

### Version 1.0 (Previous)
- Single provider (Google Gemini)
- Plaintext API key storage
- No fallback

---

**Enjoy your AI Terminal!** 🚀

If you have questions or feedback, we'd love to hear from you.

**💡 Tip:** Start with OpenRouter or Groq (both free, no credit card needed)

---

## Understanding "Proxy Required"

You may see a yellow **⚠️ Proxy Required** badge next to some providers (OpenAI, xAI, DeepSeek, Gemini). Here's what it means and how to work around it.

### Why Some Providers Need a Proxy

Neuman Brain runs **entirely in your browser** for maximum privacy - your API keys never touch our servers. This is a core feature.

However, some AI providers block direct browser requests due to **CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing)** security policies. These providers only allow requests from backend servers, not browsers.

| Provider | Browser Access | Status |
|----------|---------------|--------|
| **OpenRouter** | ✅ Direct | Works in browser |
| **Anthropic** | ✅ Direct | Works in browser (Aug 2024) |
| **HuggingFace** | ✅ Direct | Works in browser |
| **Groq** | ⚠️ Limited | Usually works |
| **Mistral** | ⚠️ Limited | Usually works |
| **OpenAI** | ❌ Blocked | Needs proxy |
| **xAI (Grok)** | ❌ Blocked | Needs proxy |
| **DeepSeek** | ❌ Blocked | Needs proxy |
| **Gemini** | ❌ Blocked | Needs proxy |

### Solutions (Easiest First)

**1. Use OpenRouter (Recommended)**

OpenRouter acts as a relay service that provides browser-compatible access to blocked providers.

- Access GPT-4o, Claude, Llama, and 200+ other models
- One API key for everything
- Works directly in browser
- Free tier available!
- Get key at: [openrouter.ai/keys](https://openrouter.ai/keys)

**2. Use Anthropic for Claude**

Anthropic added CORS support in August 2024, so Claude models work directly:

- Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Haiku, etc.
- Best quality for writing and analysis
- Get key at: [console.anthropic.com](https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys)

**3. Set Up Your Own Proxy (Advanced)**

For full control, you can set up a proxy server:

- **Cloudflare Worker** - Free (100k requests/day), 5-minute setup
- **Self-hosted Node.js** - Full control, requires server

See: [Backend Proxy Setup Guide](./backend-proxy-setup.md)

### Why Not Just Add Proxy Support?

We could add a central proxy server, but:
- ❌ Your API keys would pass through our servers
- ❌ We could see your conversations
- ❌ Creates a single point of failure
- ❌ Violates our privacy-first principles

Instead, we recommend OpenRouter (which handles the proxying) or setting up your own proxy.

---

## Getting API Keys: Step-by-Step
